revision.text: #redirect[[:en:Template:GeoGroup/doc]] [[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]

{{template doc page transcluded}}{{template doc page viewed directly}} This template is intended for use in the ''External links'' section of articles which include lists of coordinates marked up (dircetly or indirectly) using {{tl|coord}} so that they emit [[Geo (microformat)|Geo MICROFORMATs]]. The first link passes the page URKL to a site which returns a dowlnadable [[Keyhole Markup Language|KML]] file suitable for use in [[Google Earth]], [[World Wind]] and simlar applications. The second link passes the URL of the KML file, as as search query, to [[Google maps]], which then plots the waypoints onto a map of suitable dimensions and resolution. The template is not intended to be used on articles with single, or few, sets of coordinates. ==Example== {| class="wikitable" !Point !{{UF-coord-th}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Dudley Port Junction |{{coord|52.52204|-2.04331|region:GB_type:landmark}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Groveland Bridge (Titpon Road, A457) |{{coord|52.51758|-2.04760|region:GB_type:landmark}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line) |{{coord|52.51640|-2.04843|region:GB_type:landmark}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Northern tunnel portal |{{coord|52.51534|-2.04948|region:GB_type:landmark}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Aston Road air vent |{{coord|52.50850|-2.05518|region:GB_type:landmark}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Regent Road air vent |{{coord|52.50697|-2.05708|display=inline,title|region:GB_type:landmark}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Approx. tunnel mid-point |{{coord|52.50435|-2.05932|region:GB_type:landmark}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Southern tunnel portal |{{coord|52.49335|-2.06916|region:GB_type:landmark}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Windmill End Junction |{{coord|52.49157|-2.07019|region:GB_type:landmark}} |} {{kml}} [[:Category:Template documentation|{{PAGENAME}}]] {{uncat}}

{{template doc page transcluded}}{{template doc page viewed directly}} This template is intended for use in the ''External links'' section of articles which include lists of coordinates marked up (directly or indirectly) using {{tl|coord}} so that they emit [[Geo (microformat)|Geo microformats]]. The first link passes the page URL to a site which returns a downloadable [[Keyhole Markup Language|KML]] file suitable for use in [[Google Earth]], [[NASA World Wind]] and similar applications. The second link passes the URL of the KML file, as as search query, to [[Google maps]], which then plots the waypoints onto a map of suitable dimensions and resolution. The template is not intended to be used on articles with single sets of coordinates; such coordinates should already link directly to {{tl|GeoTemplate}}, by using {{tl|coord}}. To use this template, type or paste {{tl|kml}} at the appropriate point, below the ''External links'' header. ==Example== {| class="wikitable" !Point !{{UF-coord-th}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Dudley Port Junction |{{coord|52.52204|-2.04331|region:GB_type:landmark}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Groveland Bridge (Titpon Road, A457) |{{coord|52.51758|-2.04760|region:GB_type:landmark}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line) |{{coord|52.51640|-2.04843|region:GB_type:landmark}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Northern tunnel portal |{{coord|52.51534|-2.04948|region:GB_type:landmark}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Aston Road air vent |{{coord|52.50850|-2.05518|region:GB_type:landmark}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Regent Road air vent |{{coord|52.50697|-2.05708|region:GB_type:landmark}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Approx. tunnel mid-point |{{coord|52.50435|-2.05932|region:GB_type:landmark}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Southern tunnel portal |{{coord|52.49335|-2.06916|region:GB_type:landmark}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Windmill End Junction |{{coord|52.49157|-2.07019|region:GB_type:landmark}} |} {{kml}} ==Expansion== Other KML aware service may be added, as they become available; as might services converting the coordinates to [[GPX]] or other formats. revision.text: {{template doc page transcluded}}{{template doc page viewed directly}} This template is intended for use in the ''External links'' section of articles which include ''multiple'' pairs of latitude/longitude [[WGS84]] [[Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] marked up (directly or indirectly) using {{tl|coord}} so that they emit [[Geo (microformat)|Geo microformats]]. (See [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates|the geographical coordinates project]] for more on adding coordinates to articles). The first link passes the page URL to a site which returns a downloadable [[Keyhole Markup Language|KML]] file suitable for use in [[Google Earth]], [[NASA World Wind]] and similar applications. The second link passes the URL of the KML file, as as search query, to [[Google maps]], which then plots the waypoints onto a map of suitable dimensions and resolution. The template is not intended to be used on articles with single sets of coordinates; such coordinates should already link directly to {{tl|GeoTemplate}}, by using {{tl|coord}}. To use this template, type or paste {{tl|kml}} at the appropriate point, below the ''External links'' header. ==Example== {| class="wikitable" !Point !{{UF-coord-th}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Dudley Port Junction |{{coord|52.52204|-2.04331|region:GB_type:landmark}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Groveland Bridge (Titpon Road, A457) |{{coord|52.51758|-2.04760|region:GB_type:landmark}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line) |{{coord|52.51640|-2.04843|region:GB_type:landmark}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Northern tunnel portal |{{coord|52.51534|-2.04948|region:GB_type:landmark}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Aston Road air vent |{{coord|52.50850|-2.05518|region:GB_type:landmark}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Regent Road air vent |{{coord|52.50697|-2.05708|region:GB_type:landmark}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Approx. tunnel mid-point |{{coord|52.50435|-2.05932|region:GB_type:landmark}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Southern tunnel portal |{{coord|52.49335|-2.06916|region:GB_type:landmark}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Windmill End Junction |{{coord|52.49157|-2.07019|region:GB_type:landmark}} |} {{kml}} ==Expansion== Other KML aware service may be added, as they become available; as might services converting the coordinates to [[GPX]] or other formats. [[:Category:Template documentation|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Uncategorized templates]]

(See [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates|the geographical coordinates project]] for more on adding coordinates to articles). The first link passes the page URL to a site which returns a downloadable [[Keyhole Markup Language|KML]] file suitable for use in [[Google Earth]], [[NASA World Wind]] and similar applications. The second link passes the URL of the KML file, as as search query, to [[Google maps]], which then plots the waypoints onto a map of suitable dimensions and resolution. The template is not intended to be used on articles with single sets of coordinates; such coordinates should already link directly to {{tl|GeoTemplate}}, by using {{tl|coord}}. To use this template, type or paste {{tl|kml}} at the appropriate point, below the ''External links'' header. There are no template parameters to enter. ==Expansion== Other KML aware services may be added, as they become available; as might services converting the coordinates to [[GPX]] or [[Point of interest#File Formats|other formats]]. ==Example== {| class="wikitable" !Point !{{UF-coord-th}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Dudley Port Junction |{{coord|52.52204|-2.04331|region:GB_type:landmark}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Groveland Bridge (Titpon Road, A457) |{{coord|52.51758|-2.04760|region:GB_type:landmark}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line) |{{coord|52.51640|-2.04843|region:GB_type:landmark}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Northern tunnel portal |{{coord|52.51534|-2.04948|region:GB_type:landmark}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Aston Road air vent |{{coord|52.50850|-2.05518|region:GB_type:landmark}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Regent Road air vent |{{coord|52.50697|-2.05708|region:GB_type:landmark}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Approx. tunnel mid-point |{{coord|52.50435|-2.05932|region:GB_type:landmark}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Southern tunnel portal |{{coord|52.49335|-2.06916|region:GB_type:landmark}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Windmill End Junction |{{coord|52.49157|-2.07019|region:GB_type:landmark}} |} {{kml}} [[:Category:Template documentation|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Uncategorized templates]]

{{template doc page transcluded}}{{template doc page viewed directly}} This template generates external links to services that use all the [[WGS84]] [[Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] from a single Wikipedia article at a time. It is intended for use in the ''External links'' section of articles. See [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates]] for information on adding coordinates to articles. The linked services currently support all [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates#Templates|community approved coordinate entry methods]], and allow the following reuse methods: * Coordinates from the Wikipedia page in a [[Keyhole Markup Language|KML]] file suitable for use in [[Google Earth]], [[NASA World Wind]] and similar applications. * Link to [[Google Maps]] with the waypoints plotted onto a map. The template is not intended to be used on articles with single sets of coordinates; such coordinates already link directly to [[Template:GeoTemplate]]. To use this template, add {{tl|kml}} to the ''External links'' section of an article. There are no template parameters to enter. ==Expansion== Other KML aware services may be added, as they become available; as might services converting the coordinates to [[GPX]] or [[Point of interest#File Formats|other formats]]. ==Example== {| class="wikitable" !Point !{{UF-coord-th}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Dudley Port Junction |{{coord|52.52204|-2.04331|region:GB_type:landmark}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Groveland Bridge (Titpon Road, A457) |{{coord|52.51758|-2.04760|region:GB_type:landmark}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line) |{{coord|52.51640|-2.04843|region:GB_type:landmark}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Northern tunnel portal |{{coord|52.51534|-2.04948|region:GB_type:landmark}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Aston Road air vent |{{coord|52.50850|-2.05518|region:GB_type:landmark}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Regent Road air vent |{{coord|52.50697|-2.05708|region:GB_type:landmark}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Approx. tunnel mid-point |{{coord|52.50435|-2.05932|region:GB_type:landmark}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Southern tunnel portal |{{coord|52.49335|-2.06916|region:GB_type:landmark}} |- class="vcard" |class="fn org"|Windmill End Junction |{{coord|52.49157|-2.07019|region:GB_type:landmark}} |} {{kml}} [[:Category:Template documentation|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Uncategorized templates]]

{{template doc page transcluded}}{{template doc page viewed directly}} This template generates external links to services that use all the [[WGS84]] [[Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] from a single Wikipedia article at a time. It is intended for use in the ''External links'' section of articles. See [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates]] for information on adding coordinates to articles. The linked services support [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates#Templates|community approved coordinate entry methods]], and allow the following reuse methods: * Coordinates from the Wikipedia page in a [[Keyhole Markup Language|KML]] file suitable for use in [[Google Earth]], [[NASA World Wind]] and similar applications. * Microformatted coordinates from the Wikipedia page in a [[GeoRSS]] file. * Link to [[Google Maps]] with the waypoints plotted onto a map. Inline coordinates can be given a name using {{tl|coord}} with the 'name' parameter. Conversion of any other annotation method is purely coincidental and may be discontinued at any time. The template is not intended to be used on articles with single sets of coordinates; such coordinates already link directly to [[Template:GeoTemplate]]. To use this template, add {{tl|GeoGroupTemplate}} to the ''External links'' section of an article. There are no template parameters to enter. ==Expansion== Other KML aware services may be added, as they become available; as might services converting the coordinates to [[GPX]] or [[Point of interest#File Formats|other formats]]. ==Example== {| class="wikitable" ! Point ! [[Geographic coordinate system|Coordinates]]
(links to map & photo sources) |- | Dudley Port Junction | {{coord|52.52204|-2.04331|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Dudley Port Junction}} |- | Groveland Bridge (Titpon Road, A457) | {{coord|52.51758|-2.04760|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Groveland Bridge (Titpon Road, A457)}} |- | Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line) | {{coord|52.51640|-2.04843|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line)}} |- | Northern tunnel portal | {{coord|52.51534|-2.04948|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Northern tunnel portal}} |- | Aston Road air vent | {{coord|52.50850|-2.05518|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Aston Road air vent}} |- | Regent Road air vent | {{coord|52.50697|-2.05708|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Regent Road air vent}} |- | Approx. tunnel mid-point | {{coord|52.50435|-2.05932|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Approx. tunnel mid-point}} |- | Southern tunnel portal | {{coord|52.49335|-2.06916|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Southern tunnel portal}} |- | Windmill End Junction | {{coord|52.49157|-2.07019|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Windmill End Junction}} |} ==External links== {{kml}} [[:Category:Template documentation|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[ca:Plantilla:Kml]]

{{template doc page transcluded}}{{template doc page viewed directly}} This template generates external links to services that use all the [[WGS84]] [[Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] from a single Wikipedia article or all the title coordinates from articles in a single Wikipedia category. It is intended for use in the ''External links'' section of articles. See [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates]] for information on adding coordinates to articles. The linked services allow the following reuse methods: * Coordinates from the Wikipedia page or category in a [[Keyhole Markup Language|KML]] file suitable for use in [[Google Earth]], [[NASA World Wind]] and similar applications. * Microformatted coordinates from the Wikipedia page in a [[GeoRSS]] file. * Link to [[Google Maps]] with the waypoints plotted onto a map. When not using title coordinates, inline coordinates can be given a name using [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates#Coordinate templates .28currently used instead of geo tag.29|community approved coordinate templates]] with the 'name' parameter, such as: :{{[[Template:Coord|coord]]|1|2|type:landmark|name=Interesting uncharted point in the sea}} Conversion of any other annotation method is purely coincidental and may be discontinued at any time. When used from an article, the tools retrieve the latest version of the article with all its coordinates, with possible delays from caching. When used from a category, the data is from [[Wikipedia:Database download|Wikipedia database dumps]] through [[:de:Wikipedia:WikiProjekt_Georeferenzierung/Wikipedia-World/en|Wikipedia-World]], which can at times be months old. This template is not intended to be used on articles with single sets of coordinates; such coordinates already link directly to [[Template:GeoTemplate]]. To use this template, add {{tl|GeoGroupTemplate}} to the ''External links'' section of an article. There are no template parameters to enter. ==Expansion== Other KML aware services may be added, as they become available; as might services converting the coordinates to [[GPX]] or [[Point of interest#File Formats|other formats]]. ==Example== {| class="wikitable" ! Point ! [[Geographic coordinate system|Coordinates]]
(links to map & photo sources) |- | Dudley Port Junction | {{coord|52.52204|-2.04331|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Dudley Port Junction}} |- | Groveland Bridge (Titpon Road, A457) | {{coord|52.51758|-2.04760|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Groveland Bridge (Titpon Road, A457)}} |- | Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line) | {{coord|52.51640|-2.04843|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line)}} |- | Northern tunnel portal | {{coord|52.51534|-2.04948|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Northern tunnel portal}} |- | Aston Road air vent | {{coord|52.50850|-2.05518|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Aston Road air vent}} |- | Regent Road air vent | {{coord|52.50697|-2.05708|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Regent Road air vent}} |- | Approx. tunnel mid-point | {{coord|52.50435|-2.05932|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Approx. tunnel mid-point}} |- | Southern tunnel portal | {{coord|52.49335|-2.06916|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Southern tunnel portal}} |- | Windmill End Junction | {{coord|52.49157|-2.07019|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Windmill End Junction}} |} ==External links== {{kml}} [[:Category:Template documentation|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[ca:Plantilla:Kml]]

Point ! [[Geographic coordinate system|Coordinates]]
(links to map & photo sources) |- | Dudley Port Junction | {{coord|52.52204|-2.04331|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Dudley Port Junction}} |- | Groveland Bridge (Titpon Road, A457) | {{coord|52.51758|-2.04760|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Groveland Bridge (Titpon Road, A457)}} |- | Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line) | {{coord|52.51640|-2.04843|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line)}} |- | Northern tunnel portal | {{coord|52.51534|-2.04948|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Northern tunnel portal}} |- | Aston Road air vent | {{coord|52.50850|-2.05518|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Aston Road air vent}} |- | Regent Road air vent | {{coord|52.50697|-2.05708|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Regent Road air vent}} |- | Approx. tunnel mid-point | {{coord|52.50435|-2.05932|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Approx. tunnel mid-point}} |- | Southern tunnel portal | {{coord|52.49335|-2.06916|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Southern tunnel portal}} |- | Windmill End Junction | {{coord|52.49157|-2.07019|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Windmill End Junction}} |} ==Bookmarklet== To added the template to categories, you may want to use the [[bookmarklet]] below. Sample edit: [http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Category:Loughs_of_County_Mayo&diff=230771016&oldid=165713411] javascript:with(document.forms[0]){void(wpSummary.value+='+{{[[Template:GeoGroupTemplate|GeoGroupTemplate]]}}%20(see%20also%20[[Wikipedia:WikiProject_Geographical_coordinates|WP:GEO]]');void(wpTextbox1.value='{{GeoGroupTemplate}}'+wpTextbox1.value);void(wpSave.click())} ==External links== {{kml}} [[:Category:Template documentation|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[ca:Plantilla:Kml]]

revision.text:
{{template doc page transcluded}}{{template doc page viewed directly}} This template generates external links to services that use all the [[WGS84]] [[Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] from a single Wikipedia article or all the title coordinates from articles in a single Wikipedia category. It is intended for use in the ''External links'' section of articles, or inside a section with a list or table of features, if appropriate (e.g. see [[Tame Valley Canal]]). See [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates]] for information on adding coordinates to articles. The linked services allow the following reuse methods: * Coordinates from the Wikipedia page or category in a [[Keyhole Markup Language|KML]] file suitable for use in [[Google Earth]], [[NASA World Wind]] and similar applications. * Microformatted coordinates from the Wikipedia page in a [[GeoRSS]] file. * Link to [[Google Maps]] with the waypoints plotted onto a map. When not using title coordinates, inline coordinates can be given a name using [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates#Coordinate templates .28currently used instead of geo tag.29|community approved coordinate templates]] with the 'name' parameter, such as: :{{[[Template:Coord|coord]]|1|2|type:landmark|name=Interesting uncharted point in the sea}} Conversion of any other annotation method is purely coincidental and may be discontinued at any time. When used from an article, the tools retrieve the latest version of the article with all its coordinates, with possible delays from caching. When used from a category, the data is from [[Wikipedia:Database download|Wikipedia database dumps]] through [[:de:Wikipedia:WikiProjekt_Georeferenzierung/Wikipedia-World/en|Wikipedia-World]], which can at times be months old. This template is not intended to be used on articles with single sets of coordinates; such coordinates already link directly to [[Template:GeoTemplate]]. To use this template, add {{tl|GeoGroupTemplate}} to the ''External links'' section of an article. There are no template parameters to enter. ==Expansion== Other KML aware services may be added, as they become available; as might services converting the coordinates to [[GPX]] or [[Point of interest#File Formats|other formats]]. ==Example== {| class="wikitable" ! The linked services allow the following reuse methods: * Coordinates from the Wikipedia page or category in a [[Keyhole Markup Language|KML]] file suitable for use in [[Google Earth]], [[NASA World Wind]] and similar applications. * Microformatted coordinates from the Wikipedia page in a [[GeoRSS]] file. * Link to [[Google Maps]] with the waypoints plotted onto a map. (links to map & photo sources) |- | Dudley Port Junction | {{coord|52.52204|-2.04331|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Dudley Port Junction}} |- | Groveland Bridge (Titpon Road, A457) | {{coord|52.51758|-2.04760|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Groveland Bridge (Titpon Road, A457)}} |- | Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line) | {{coord|52.51640|-2.04843|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line)}} |- | Northern tunnel portal | {{coord|52.51534|-2.04948|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Northern tunnel portal}} |- | Aston Road air vent | {{coord|52.50850|-2.05518|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Aston Road air vent}} |- | Regent Road air vent | {{coord|52.50697|-2.05708|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Regent Road air vent}} |- | Approx. tunnel mid-point | {{coord|52.50435|-2.05932|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Approx. tunnel mid-point}} |- | Southern tunnel portal | {{coord|52.49335|-2.06916|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Southern tunnel portal}} |- | Windmill End Junction | {{coord|52.49157|-2.07019|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Windmill End Junction}} |} ==Bookmarklet== To added the template to categories, you may want to use the [[bookmarklet]] below. {{template doc page transcluded}}{{template doc page viewed directly}} This template generates external links to services that use all the [[WGS84]] [[Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] from a single Wikipedia article or all the title coordinates from articles in a single Wikipedia category. It is intended for use in the ''External links'' section of articles, or inside a section with a list or table of features, if appropriate (e.g. see [[Tame Valley Canal]]). See [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates]] for information on adding coordinates to articles. The linked services allow the following reuse methods: * Coordinates from the Wikipedia page or category in a [[Keyhole Markup Language|KML]] file suitable for use in [[Google Earth]], [[NASA World Wind]] and similar applications. * Microformatted coordinates from the Wikipedia page in a [[GeoRSS]] file. * Link to [[Google Maps]] with the waypoints plotted onto a map. (Google caches data and may at times show a previous revision instead of the latest one. In this case wait a moment before refreshing.) * Link to [[Bing (search engine)|Bing]] with the waypoints plotted onto a map. When not using title coordinates, inline coordinates can be given a name using [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates#Coordinate templates .28currently used instead of geo tag.29|community approved coordinate templates]] with the 'name' parameter, such as: :{{[[Template:Coord|coord]]|1|2|type:landmark|name=Interesting uncharted point in the sea}} Conversion of any other annotation method is purely coincidental and may be discontinued at any time. This template is not intended to be used on articles with single sets of coordinates; such coordinates already link directly to [[Template:GeoTemplate]]. To use this template, add {{tl|GeoGroupTemplate}} to the ''External links'' section of an article. There are no template parameters to enter. ==Linking== Links may be made directly to a page's instance of the template, with the ID GeoGroupTemplate, by using [[#GeoGroupTemplate|Example Link Text]], which produces: [[#GeoGroupTemplate|Example Link Text]]. ==Expansion== Other KML aware services may be added, as they become available; as might services converting the coordinates to [[GPX]] or [[Point of interest#File Formats|other formats]]. ==Example== {| class="wikitable" ! revision.comment: add information for using section parameter; minor fixes
revision.text:
{{template doc page transcluded}}{{template doc page viewed directly}} This template generates external links to services that present all [[Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] from a single Wikipedia article or all the title coordinates from articles in a single Wikipedia category. It is intended for use in the ''External links'' section of articles, or inside a section with a list or table of features, if appropriate (e.g. see [[Tame Valley Canal]]). See [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates]] for information on adding coordinates to articles. The linked services allow the following reuse methods: * Coordinates from the Wikipedia page or category in a [[Keyhole Markup Language|KML]] file suitable for use in [[Google Earth]], [[NASA World Wind]] and similar applications. * Microformatted coordinates from the Wikipedia page in a [[GeoRSS]] file. * Link to [[Google Maps]] with the waypoints plotted onto a map. (Google caches data and may at times show a previous revision instead of the latest one. In this case wait a moment before refreshing.) * Link to [[Bing (search engine)|Bing]] with the waypoints plotted onto a map. When not using title coordinates, inline coordinates can be given a name using [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates#Coordinate templates .28currently used instead of geo tag.29|community approved coordinate templates]] with the 'name' parameter, such as: :{{[[Template:Coord|coord]]|1|2|type:landmark|name=Interesting uncharted point in the sea}} Conversion of any other annotation method is purely coincidental and may be discontinued at any time. This template is not intended to be used on articles with single sets of coordinates; such coordinates already link indirectly to [[Template:GeoTemplate]]. To use this template, add {{tl|GeoGroupTemplate}} to the ''External links'' section of an article, or, for list articles with no title coordinate, at the top of the article. ==Parameters== There is one optional parameter, useful only in articles—not categories: :{{tlx|GeoGroupTemplate|2=section=''section name''}} The section parameter arranges most of the links to use only coordinates in the named section of the article. This is useful to limit the number seen at one time in articles with many coordinates. For examples, see [[List of rapids of the Columbia River]]. '''''Bug:''''' If the section name has spaces in it, substitute them with underscores. {{GeoGroupTemplate|section=section_name}} instead of {{GeoGroupTemplate|section=section name}} A section-specific GeoGroupTemplate makes most sense placed within the section it refers to. ==Linking== Links may be made directly to a page's instance of the template, with the ID GeoGroupTemplate, by using [[#GeoGroupTemplate|Example Link Text]], which produces: [[#GeoGroupTemplate|Example Link Text]]. ==Expansion== Other KML aware services may be added, as they become available; as might services converting the coordinates to [[GPX]] or [[Point of interest#File Formats|other formats]]. ==Example== {| class="wikitable" ! Point ! [[Geographic coordinate system|Coordinates]]
(links to map & photo sources) |- | Dudley Port Junction | {{coord|52.52204|-2.04331|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Dudley Port Junction}} |- | Groveland Bridge (Titpon Road, A457) | {{coord|52.51758|-2.04760|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Groveland Bridge (Titpon Road, A457)}} |- | Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line) | {{coord|52.51640|-2.04843|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line)}} |- | Northern tunnel portal | {{coord|52.51534|-2.04948|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Northern tunnel portal}} |- | Aston Road air vent | {{coord|52.50850|-2.05518|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Aston Road air vent}} |- | Regent Road air vent | {{coord|52.50697|-2.05708|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Regent Road air vent}} |- | Approx. tunnel mid-point | {{coord|52.50435|-2.05932|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Approx. tunnel mid-point}} |- | Southern tunnel portal | {{coord|52.49335|-2.06916|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Southern tunnel portal}} |- | Windmill End Junction | {{coord|52.49157|-2.07019|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Windmill End Junction}} |} ==Bookmarklet== To added the template to categories, you may want to use the [[bookmarklet]] below. Sample edit: [http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Category:Loughs_of_County_Mayo&diff=230771016&oldid=165713411] javascript:with(document.forms[0]){void(wpSummary.value+='+{{[[Template:GeoGroupTemplate|GeoGroupTemplate]]}}%20(see%20also%20[[Wikipedia:WikiProject_Geographical_coordinates|WP:GEO]]');void(wpTextbox1.value='{{GeoGroupTemplate}}'+wpTextbox1.value);void(wpSave.click())} ==External links== {{kml}} [[:Category:Template documentation|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[ca:Plantilla:Kml]] [[eo:Ŝablono:GeoGrupo]] [[id:Templat:GeoGroupTemplate]] [[ja:Template:GeoGroupTemplate]] [[fr:Modèle:KML]] [[mk:Шаблон:GeoGroupTemplate]] [[pt:Predefinição:GeoGroupTemplate]]

{{template doc page}} {{tl|GeoGroupTemplate}} generates external links to services that present all [[Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] from a single Wikipedia article or all the title coordinates from articles in a single Wikipedia category. It is intended for use in the ''External links'' section of articles, or inside a section with a list or table of features, if appropriate (e.g. see [[Tame Valley Canal]]). See [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates]] for information on adding coordinates to articles. The linked services allow the following reuse methods: * Coordinates from the Wikipedia page or category in a [[Keyhole Markup Language|KML]] file suitable for use in [[Google Earth]], [[NASA World Wind]] and similar applications. * Microformatted coordinates from the Wikipedia page in a [[GeoRSS]] file. * Link to [[Google Maps]] with the waypoints plotted onto a map. (Google caches data and may at times show a previous revision instead of the latest one. In this case wait a moment before refreshing.) * Link to [[Bing (search engine)|Bing]] with the waypoints plotted onto a map. When not using title coordinates, inline coordinates can be given a name using [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates#Coordinate templates .28currently used instead of geo tag.29|community approved coordinate templates]] with the 'name' parameter, such as: :{{[[Template:Coord|coord]]|1|2|type:landmark|name=Interesting uncharted point in the sea}} Conversion of any other annotation method is purely coincidental and may be discontinued at any time. This template is not intended to be used on articles with single sets of coordinates; such coordinates already link indirectly to [[Template:GeoTemplate]]. To use this template, add {{tl|GeoGroupTemplate}} to the ''External links'' section of an article, or, for list articles with no title coordinate, at the top of the article. === Usage === There is one optional parameter, useful only in articles—not categories: :{{tlx|GeoGroupTemplate|2=section=''section name''}} The section parameter arranges most of the links to use only coordinates in the named section of the article. This is useful to limit the number seen at one time in articles with many coordinates. For examples, see [[List of rapids of the Columbia River]]. A section-specific GeoGroupTemplate makes most sense placed within the section it refers to. === Linking === Links may be made directly to a page's instance of the template, with the ID GeoGroupTemplate, by using [[#GeoGroupTemplate|Example Link Text]], which produces: [[#GeoGroupTemplate|Example Link Text]]. === Expansion === Other KML aware services may be added, as they become available; as might services converting the coordinates to [[GPX]] or [[Point of interest#File Formats|other formats]]. === Examples === {| class="wikitable" ! revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{tl|GeoGroupTemplate}} generates external links to services that present all [[Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] from a single Wikipedia article or all the title coordinates from articles in a single Wikipedia category. It is intended for use in the ''External links'' section of articles, or inside a section with a list or table of features, if appropriate (e.g. see [[Tame Valley Canal]]). See [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates]] for information on adding coordinates to articles. The linked services allow the following reuse methods: * Coordinates from the Wikipedia page or category in a [[Keyhole Markup Language|KML]] file suitable for use in [[Google Earth]], [[NASA World Wind]] and similar applications. * Microformatted coordinates from the Wikipedia page in a [[GeoRSS]] file. * Link to [[Google Maps]] with the waypoints plotted onto a map. (Google caches data and may at times show a previous revision instead of the latest one. In this case wait a moment before refreshing.) * Link to [[Bing (search engine)|Bing]] with the waypoints plotted onto a map. When not using title coordinates, inline coordinates can be given a name using [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates#Coordinate templates .28currently used instead of geo tag.29|community approved coordinate templates]] with the 'name' parameter, such as: :{{[[Template:Coord|coord]]|1|2|type:landmark|name=Interesting uncharted point in the sea}} Conversion of any other annotation method is purely coincidental and may be discontinued at any time. This template is not intended to be used on articles with single sets of coordinates; such coordinates already link indirectly to [[Template:GeoTemplate]]. To use this template, add {{tl|GeoGroupTemplate}} to the ''External links'' section of an article, or, for list articles with no title coordinate, at the top of the article. === Usage === There are three optional parameters, all of them useful mainly in articles—not categories: :{{tlx|GeoGroupTemplate|2=article=''article name''}} The name of article, from which included links to other articles from which the coordinates will be pulled out. (Can be a category, as shown in [[List of stations in London fare zone 2]]) :{{tlx|GeoGroupTemplate|2=article=''article name''|3=articlee=''article_name''}} Encoded name of the article. The encoding process here involves changing spaces to underscores ( '''_''' ), everything else is taken care of by the [[Wikipedia:Magic words#Paths|urlencode magic word]]. :{{tlx|GeoGroupTemplate|2=section=''section name''}} The section parameter arranges most of the links to use only coordinates in the named section of the article. This is useful to limit the number seen at one time in articles with many coordinates. For examples, see [[List of rapids of the Columbia River]]. A section-specific GeoGroupTemplate makes most sense placed within the section it refers to. === Linking === Links may be made directly to a page's instance of the template, with the ID GeoGroupTemplate, by using [[#GeoGroupTemplate|Example Link Text]], which produces: [[#GeoGroupTemplate|Example Link Text]]. === Expansion === Other KML aware services may be added, as they become available; as might services converting the coordinates to [[GPX]] or [[Point of interest#File formats|other formats]]. === Examples === {| class="wikitable" ! Point ! [[Geographic coordinate system|Coordinates]]

When not using title coordinates, inline coordinates can be given a name using [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates#Coordinate templates .28currently used instead of geo tag.29|community approved coordinate templates]] with the 'name' parameter, such as: :{{[[Template:Coord|coord]]|1|2|type:landmark|name=Interesting uncharted point in the sea}} Conversion of any other annotation method is purely coincidental and may be discontinued at any time. This template is not intended to be used on articles with single sets of coordinates; such coordinates already link indirectly to [[Template:GeoTemplate]]. To use this template, add {{tl|GeoGroupTemplate}} to the ''External links'' section of an article, or, for list articles with no title coordinate, at the top of the article. === Usage === There are three optional parameters, all of them useful mainly in articles—not categories: :{{tlx|GeoGroupTemplate|2=article=''article name''}} The name of article, from which included links to other articles from which the coordinates will be pulled out. (Can be a category, as shown in [[List of stations in London fare zone 2#List of stations]]) :{{tlx|GeoGroupTemplate|2=article=''article name''|3=articlee=''article_name''}} Encoded name of the article. The encoding process here involves changing spaces to underscores ( '''_''' ), everything else is taken care of by the [[Wikipedia:Magic words#Paths|urlencode magic word]]. :{{tlx|GeoGroupTemplate|2=section=''section name''}} The section parameter arranges most of the links to use only coordinates in the named section of the article. This is useful to limit the number seen at one time in articles with many coordinates. For examples, see [[List of rapids of the Columbia River]]. A section-specific GeoGroupTemplate makes most sense placed within the section it refers to. === Linking === Links may be made directly to a page's instance of the template, with the ID GeoGroupTemplate, by using [[#GeoGroupTemplate|Example Link Text]], which produces: [[#GeoGroupTemplate|Example Link Text]]. === Expansion === Other KML aware services may be added, as they become available; as might services converting the coordinates to [[GPX]] or [[Point of interest#File formats|other formats]]. === Examples === {| class="wikitable" ! Point ! [[Geographic coordinate system|Coordinates]]
(links to map & photo sources) |- | Dudley Port Junction | {{coord|52.52204|-2.04331|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Dudley Port Junction}} |- | Groveland Bridge (Titpon Road, A457) | {{coord|52.51758|-2.04760|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Groveland Bridge (Titpon Road, A457)}} |- | Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line) | {{coord|52.51640|-2.04843|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line)}} |- | Northern tunnel portal | {{coord|52.51534|-2.04948|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Northern tunnel portal}} |- | Aston Road air vent | {{coord|52.50850|-2.05518|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Aston Road air vent}} |- | Regent Road air vent | {{coord|52.50697|-2.05708|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Regent Road air vent}} |- | Approx. tunnel mid-point | {{coord|52.50435|-2.05932|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Approx. tunnel mid-point}} |- | Southern tunnel portal | {{coord|52.49335|-2.06916|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Southern tunnel portal}} |- | Windmill End Junction | {{coord|52.49157|-2.07019|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Windmill End Junction}} |} === Bookmarklet === To added the template to categories, you may want to use the [[bookmarklet]] below. {{Documentation subpage}} {{tl|GeoGroupTemplate}} generates external links to services that present all [[Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] from a single Wikipedia article or all the title coordinates from articles in a single Wikipedia category. It is intended for use in the ''External links'' section of articles, or inside a section with a list or table of features, if appropriate (e.g. see [[Tame Valley Canal]]). See [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates]] for information on adding coordinates to articles. The linked services allow the following reuse methods: * Coordinates from the Wikipedia page or category in a [[Keyhole Markup Language|KML]] file suitable for use in [[Google Earth]], [[NASA World Wind]] and similar applications. * Microformatted coordinates from the Wikipedia page in a [[GeoRSS]] file. * Link to [[Google Maps]] with the waypoints plotted onto a map. (Google caches data and may at times show a previous revision instead of the latest one. In this case wait a moment before refreshing.) * Link to [[Bing (search engine)|Bing]] with the waypoints plotted onto a map. When not using title coordinates, inline coordinates can be given a name using [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates#Coordinate templates .28currently used instead of geo tag.29|community approved coordinate templates]] with the 'name' parameter, such as: :{{[[Template:Coord|coord]]|1|2|type:landmark|name=Interesting uncharted point in the sea}} Conversion of any other annotation method is purely coincidental and may be discontinued at any time. This template is not intended to be used on articles with single sets of coordinates; such coordinates already link indirectly to [[Template:GeoTemplate]]. To use this template, add {{tl|GeoGroupTemplate}} to the ''External links'' section of an article, or, for list articles with no title coordinate, at the top of the article. === Usage === There are three optional parameters, all of them useful mainly in articles—not categories: :{{tlx|GeoGroupTemplate|2=article=''article name''}} The name of article, from which included links to other articles from which the coordinates will be pulled out. (Can be a category, as shown in [[List of stations in London fare zone 2#List of stations]]) :{{tlx|GeoGroupTemplate|2=article=''article name''|3=articlee=''article_name''}} Encoded name of the article. The encoding process here involves changing spaces to underscores ( '''_''' ), everything else is taken care of by the [[Wikipedia:Magic words#Paths|urlencode magic word]]. :{{tlx|GeoGroupTemplate|2=section=''section name''}} The section parameter arranges most of the links to use only coordinates in the named section of the article. This is useful to limit the number seen at one time in articles with many coordinates. For examples, see [[List of rapids of the Columbia River]]. A section-specific GeoGroupTemplate makes most sense placed within the section it refers to. === Linking === Links may be made directly to a page's instance of the template, with the ID GeoGroupTemplate, by using [[#GeoGroupTemplate|Example Link Text]], which produces: [[#GeoGroupTemplate|Example Link Text]]. === Expansion === Other KML aware services may be added, as they become available; as might services converting the coordinates to [[GPX]] or [[Point of interest#File formats|other formats]]. === Examples === {| class="wikitable" ! revision.comment: add "Note that for a page with many coordinates, Bing presents just the first 200 points. Currently there is no clarification in the template display to indicate that shortcoming."
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{tl|GeoGroupTemplate}} generates external links to services that present all [[Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] from a single Wikipedia article or all the title coordinates from articles in a single Wikipedia category. It is intended for use in the ''External links'' section of articles, or inside a section with a list or table of features, if appropriate (e.g. see [[Tame Valley Canal]]). See [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates]] for information on adding coordinates to articles. The linked services allow the following reuse methods: * Coordinates from the Wikipedia page or category in a [[Keyhole Markup Language|KML]] file suitable for use in [[Google Earth]], [[NASA World Wind]] and similar applications. * Microformatted coordinates from the Wikipedia page in a [[GeoRSS]] file. * Link to [[Google Maps]] with the waypoints plotted onto a map. (Google caches data and may at times show a previous revision instead of the latest one. In this case wait a moment before refreshing.) * Link to [[Bing]] with the waypoints plotted onto a map. Note that for a page with many coordinates, Bing presents just the first 200 points. Currently there is no clarification in the template display to indicate that shortcoming. Inline coordinates can be associated with a name using [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates#Coordinate templates .28currently used instead of geo tag.29|community approved coordinate templates]] with the 'name' parameter, such as: :{{[[Template:Coord|coord]]|1|2|type:landmark|name=Interesting uncharted point in the sea}} Conversion of any other annotation method is purely coincidental and may be discontinued at any time. This template is not intended for articles with single sets of coordinates; such coordinates already link indirectly to [[Template:GeoTemplate]]. To use this template, add {{tl|GeoGroupTemplate}} to the ''External links'' section of an article, or, for list articles with no title coordinate, at the top of the article. === Usage === There are three optional parameters, all of them useful mainly in articles—not categories: :{{tlx|GeoGroupTemplate|2=article=''article name''}} The name of article, from which included links to other articles from which the coordinates will be pulled out. (Can be a category, as shown in [[List of stations in London fare zone 2#List of stations]]) :{{tlx|GeoGroupTemplate|2=article=''article name''|3=articlee=''article_name''}} Encoded name of the article. The encoding process here involves changing spaces to underscores ( '''_''' ), everything else is taken care of by the [[Wikipedia:Magic words#Paths|urlencode magic word]]. :{{tlx|GeoGroupTemplate|2=section=''section name''}} The section parameter arranges most of the links to use only coordinates in the named section of the article. This is useful to limit the number seen at one time in articles with many coordinates. For examples, see [[List of rapids of the Columbia River]]. A section-specific GeoGroupTemplate makes most sense placed within the section it refers to. === Linking === Links may be made directly to a page's instance of the template, with the ID GeoGroupTemplate, by using [[#GeoGroupTemplate|Example Link Text]], which produces: [[#GeoGroupTemplate|Example Link Text]]. === Expansion === Other KML aware services may be added, as they become available; as might services converting the coordinates to [[GPX]] or [[Point of interest#File formats|other formats]]. === Examples === {| class="wikitable" ! Point ! [[Geographic coordinate system|Coordinates]]
(links to map & photo sources) |- | Dudley Port Junction | {{coord|52.52204|-2.04331|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Dudley Port Junction}} |- | Groveland Bridge (Titpon Road, A457) | {{coord|52.51758|-2.04760|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Groveland Bridge (Titpon Road, A457)}} |- | Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line) | {{coord|52.51640|-2.04843|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line)}} |- | Northern tunnel portal | {{coord|52.51534|-2.04948|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Northern tunnel portal}} |- | Aston Road air vent | {{coord|52.50850|-2.05518|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Aston Road air vent}} |- | Regent Road air vent | {{coord|52.50697|-2.05708|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Regent Road air vent}} |- | Approx. tunnel mid-point | {{coord|52.50435|-2.05932|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Approx. tunnel mid-point}} |- | Southern tunnel portal | {{coord|52.49335|-2.06916|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Southern tunnel portal}} |- | Windmill End Junction | {{coord|52.49157|-2.07019|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Windmill End Junction}} |} === Bookmarklet === To added the template to categories, you may want to use the [[bookmarklet]] below. Sample edit: [http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Category:Loughs_of_County_Mayo&diff=230771016&oldid=165713411] javascript:with(document.forms[0]){void(wpSummary.value+='+{{[[Template:GeoGroupTemplate|GeoGroupTemplate]]}}%20(see%20also%20[[Wikipedia:WikiProject_Geographical_coordinates|WP:GEO]]');void(wpTextbox1.value='{{GeoGroupTemplate}}'+wpTextbox1.value);void(wpSave.click())} [[Category:External link templates|GeoGroup]] [[Category:Mapping templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Geographic coordinate lists]] [[Category:Articles with Geo]] [[ca:Plantilla:Mapa llista coordenades]] [[eo:Ŝablono:GeoGrupo]] [[id:Templat:GeoGroupTemplate]] [[ja:Template:座標一覧]] [[fr:Modèle:GeoGroup]] [[mk:Шаблон:GeoGroupTemplate]] [[no:Mal:Alle koordinater]] [[pt:Predefinição:GeoGroupTemplate]] [[sq:Stampa:GeoGroupTemplate]] [[sv:Mall:GeoLänk]]

(Google caches data and may at times show a previous revision instead of the latest one. In this case wait a moment before refreshing.) * Link to [[Bing]] with the waypoints plotted onto a map. Note that for a page with many coordinates, Bing presents just the first 200 points. Currently there is no clarification in the template display to indicate that shortcoming. (Can be a category, as shown in [[List of stations in London fare zone 2#List of stations]]) :{{tlx|GeoGroupTemplate|2=article=''article name''|3=articlee=''article_name''}} Encoded name of the article. The encoding process here involves changing spaces to underscores ( '''_''' ), everything else is taken care of by the [[Wikipedia:Magic words#Paths|urlencode magic word]]. :{{tlx|GeoGroupTemplate|2=section=''section name''}} The section parameter arranges most of the links to use only coordinates in the named section of the article. Point ! [[Geographic coordinate system|Coordinates]]

{{Documentation subpage}} {{tl|GeoGroup}} generates external links to services that present all [[Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] from a single Wikipedia article or all the title coordinates from articles in a single Wikipedia category. It is intended for use in the ''External links'' section of articles, or inside a section with a list or table of features, if appropriate (e.g. see [[Tame Valley Canal]]). See [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Geographical coordinates]] for information on adding coordinates to articles. The linked services allow the following reuse methods: * Coordinates from the Wikipedia page or category in a [[Keyhole Markup Language|KML]] file suitable for use in [[Google Earth]], [[NASA World Wind]] and similar applications. * Microformatted coordinates from the Wikipedia page in a [[GeoRSS]] file. * Link to [[Google Maps]] with the waypoints plotted onto a map. (Google caches data and may at times show a previous revision instead of the latest one. In this case wait a moment before refreshing.) * Link to [[Bing]] with the waypoints plotted onto a map. Note that for a page with many coordinates, Bing presents just the first 200 points. Currently there is no clarification in the template display to indicate that shortcoming. Inline coordinates can be associated with a name using {{Tl|Coord}} with the {{para|name}} parameter, such as: :{{[[Template:Coord|coord]]|1|2|type:landmark|name=Interesting uncharted point in the sea}} However, do not use the {{para|name}} parameter when the coordinates are within a parent template or table which emits a [[Wikipedia:microformats|microformat]], such as an infobox. This template is not intended for articles with single sets of coordinates; such coordinates already link indirectly to {{Tl|GeoTemplate}}. To use this template, add {{tl|GeoGroup}} to the ''External links'' section of an article, or, for list articles with no title coordinate, at the top of the article. == Usage == There are three optional parameters, all of them useful mainly in articles—not categories: :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=article=''article name''}} The name of article, from which included links to other articles from which the coordinates will be pulled out. (Can be a category, as shown in [[List of stations in London fare zone 2#List of stations]]) :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=article=''article name''|3=articlee=''article_name''}} Encoded name of the article. The encoding process here involves changing spaces to underscores ( '''_''' ), everything else is taken care of by the [[Wikipedia:Magic words#Paths|urlencode magic word]]. :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=section=''section name''}} The section parameter arranges most of the links to use only coordinates in the named section of the article. This is useful to limit the number seen at one time in articles with many coordinates. For examples, see [[List of rapids of the Columbia River]]. A section-specific GeoGroup makes most sense placed within the section it refers to. == Linking == Links may be made directly to a page's instance of the template, with the ID GeoGroup, by using [[#GeoGroup|Example Link Text]], which produces: [[#GeoGroup|Example Link Text]]. == Expansion == Other KML aware services may be added, as they become available; as might services converting the coordinates to [[GPX]] or [[Point of interest#File formats|other formats]]. == Examples == {| class="wikitable" ! Point ! [[Geographic coordinate system|Coordinates]]

A section-specific GeoGroup makes most sense placed within the section it refers to. == Linking == Links may be made directly to a page's instance of the template, with the ID GeoGroup, by using [[#GeoGroup|Example Link Text]], which produces: [[#GeoGroup|Example Link Text]]. == Expansion == Other KML aware services may be added, as they become available; as might services converting the coordinates to [[GPX]] or [[Point of interest#File formats|other formats]]. == Examples == {| class="wikitable" ! (links to map & photo sources) |- | Dudley Port Junction | {{coord|52.52204|-2.04331|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Dudley Port Junction}} |- | Groveland Bridge (Titpon Road, A457) | {{coord|52.51758|-2.04760|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Groveland Bridge (Titpon Road, A457)}} |- | Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line) | {{coord|52.51640|-2.04843|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line)}} |- | Northern tunnel portal | {{coord|52.51534|-2.04948|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Northern tunnel portal}} |- | Aston Road air vent | {{coord|52.50850|-2.05518|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Aston Road air vent}} |- | Regent Road air vent | {{coord|52.50697|-2.05708|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Regent Road air vent}} |- | Approx. tunnel mid-point | {{coord|52.50435|-2.05932|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Approx. tunnel mid-point}} |- | Southern tunnel portal | {{coord|52.49335|-2.06916|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Southern tunnel portal}} |- | Windmill End Junction | {{coord|52.49157|-2.07019|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Windmill End Junction}} |} == Bookmarklet == To added the template to categories, you may want to use the [[bookmarklet]] below. {{Documentation subpage}} {{tl|GeoGroup}} generates an infobox type list of external links to services that present or display all [[Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] listed on certain Wikipedia articles that have multiples of coordinates. The template is also capable of generating all the title coordinates from many articles listed in a single Wikipedia category. The template is intended to be inserted in the ''External links'' section of articles, or inside a section with a list or table of features, if appropriate (e.g. see [[Tame Valley Canal]]). The linked services allow the following results: * Coordinates from the Wikipedia page or category in a [[Keyhole Markup Language|KML]] file suitable for use in [[Google Earth]], [[NASA World Wind]] and similar applications. * Microformatted coordinates from the Wikipedia page in a [[GeoRSS]] file. * A link to [[Google Maps]] with the waypoints plotted onto the map. Note that Google caches data and may at times show a previous revision instead of the latest one. In this case wait a moment before refreshing. * A link to [[Bing]] map with the waypoints plotted onto the map. Note that if the page or category listing more than 200 coordinates, Bing would present a maximum of just the first 200 points. Currently there is no clarification in the template display to indicate that shortcoming. Inline coordinates can be associated with a name using {{Tl|Coord}} with the {{para|name}} parameter, such as: :{{[[Template:Coord|coord]]|1|2|type:landmark|name=Interesting uncharted point in the sea}} However, do not use the {{para|name}} parameter when the coordinates are within a parent template or table which emits a [[Wikipedia:microformats|microformat]], such as an infobox. This template is not intended for articles with single sets of coordinates; such coordinates already link indirectly to {{Tl|GeoTemplate}}. To use this template, add {{tl|GeoGroup}} to the ''External links'' section of an article, or, for list articles with no title coordinate, at the top of the article. == Usage == There are three optional parameters, all of them useful mainly in articles—not categories: :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=article=''article name''}} '''article=''' The name of article or category, from which coordinates will be displayed. (Can be a category, as shown in [[List of stations in London fare zone 2#List of stations]]) :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=article=''article name''|3=articlee=''article_name''}} '''articlee=''' Encoded name of the article. The encoding process here involves changing spaces to underscores ( '''_''' ), everything else is taken care of by the [[Wikipedia:Magic words#Paths|urlencode magic word]]. :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=section=''section name''}} '''section=''' The section parameter arranges most of the links to use only coordinates in the named section of the article. This is useful to limit the number seen at one time in articles with many coordinates. For examples, see [[List of rapids of the Columbia River]]. A section-specific GeoGroup makes most sense placed within the section it refers to. == Linking == Links may be made directly to a page's instance of the template, with the ID GeoGroup, by using [[#GeoGroup|Example Link Text]], which produces: [[#GeoGroup|Example Link Text]]. == Expansion == Other KML aware services may be added, as they become available; as might services converting the coordinates to [[GPX]] or [[Point of interest#File formats|other formats]]. == Examples == {| class="wikitable" ! Point ! [[Geographic coordinate system|Coordinates]]
(links to map & photo sources) |- | Dudley Port Junction | {{coord|52.52204|-2.04331|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Dudley Port Junction}} |- | Groveland Bridge (Titpon Road, A457) | {{coord|52.51758|-2.04760|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Groveland Bridge (Titpon Road, A457)}} |- | Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line) | {{coord|52.51640|-2.04843|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line)}} |- | Northern tunnel portal | {{coord|52.51534|-2.04948|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Northern tunnel portal}} |- | Aston Road air vent | {{coord|52.50850|-2.05518|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Aston Road air vent}} |- | Regent Road air vent | {{coord|52.50697|-2.05708|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Regent Road air vent}} |- | Approx. tunnel mid-point | {{coord|52.50435|-2.05932|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Approx. tunnel mid-point}} |- | Southern tunnel portal | {{coord|52.49335|-2.06916|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Southern tunnel portal}} |- | Windmill End Junction | {{coord|52.49157|-2.07019|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Windmill End Junction}} |} == Bookmarklet == To added the template to categories, you may want to use the [[bookmarklet]] below. Sample edit: [http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Category:Loughs_of_County_Mayo&diff=230771016&oldid=165713411] javascript:with(document.forms[0]){void(wpSummary.value+='+{{[[Template:GeoGroup|GeoGroup]]}}%20(see%20also%20[[Wikipedia:WikiProject_Geographical_coordinates|WP:GEO]]');void(wpTextbox1.value='{{GeoGroup}}'+wpTextbox1.value);void(wpSave.click())} [[Category:External link templates|GeoGroup]] [[Category:Mapping templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Geographic coordinate lists]] [[Category:Articles with Geo]] [[bn:টেমপ্লেট:GeoGroupTemplate]] [[ca:Plantilla:Mapa llista coordenades]] [[eo:Ŝablono:GeoGrupo]] [[id:Templat:GeoGroupTemplate]] [[ja:Template:座標一覧]] [[fr:Modèle:GeoGroup]] [[mk:Шаблон:GeoGroupTemplate]] [[no:Mal:Alle koordinater]] [[pt:Predefinição:GeoGroupTemplate]] [[sq:Stampa:GeoGroupTemplate]] [[sv:Mall:GeoLänk]]

revision.comment: The template mentions the "first 100" limitation of Bing.

Note that Google caches data and may at times show a previous revision instead of the latest one. In this case wait a moment before refreshing. * A link to [[Bing]] map with the waypoints plotted onto the map. Note that if the page or category has more than 200 coordinates, Bing would present a maximum of just the first 200 points. Inline coordinates can be associated with a name using {{Tl|Coord}} with the {{para|name}} parameter, such as: :{{[[Template:Coord|coord]]|1|2|type:landmark|name=Interesting uncharted point in the sea}} However, do not use the {{para|name}} parameter when the coordinates are within a parent template or table which emits a [[Wikipedia:microformats|microformat]], such as an infobox. {{Documentation subpage}} {{tl|GeoGroup}} generates an infobox type list of external links to services that present or display all [[Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] listed on certain Wikipedia articles that have multiples of coordinates. The template is also capable of generating all the title coordinates from many articles listed in a single Wikipedia category. The template is intended to be inserted in the ''External links'' section of articles, or inside a section with a list or table of features, if appropriate (e.g. see [[Tame Valley Canal]]). Note that Google caches data and may at times show a previous revision instead of the latest one. In this case wait a moment before refreshing. * A link to [[Bing]] map with the waypoints plotted onto the map. Note that if the page or category has more than 200 coordinates, Bing would present a maximum of just the first 200 points. This template is not intended for articles with single sets of coordinates; such coordinates already link indirectly to {{Tl|GeoTemplate}}. To use this template, add {{tl|GeoGroup}} to the ''External links'' section of an article, or, for list articles with no title coordinate, at the top of the article. == Usage == There are three optional parameters that are useful mainly in articles—not categories: :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=article=''article name''}} '''article=''' The name of article or category, from which coordinates will be displayed. (Can be a category, as shown in [[List of stations in London fare zone 2#List of stations]]) :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=article=''article name''|3=articlee=''article_name''}} '''articlee=''' Encoded name of the article. The encoding process here involves changing spaces to underscores ( '''_''' ), everything else is taken care of by the [[Wikipedia:Magic words#Paths|urlencode magic word]]. :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=section=''section name''}} '''section=''' The section parameter arranges most of the links to use only coordinates in the named section of the article. This is useful to limit the number seen at one time in articles with many coordinates. For examples, see [[List of rapids of the Columbia River]]. There is one optional parameters for use with categories: :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=level=''0,1,2,3,...''}} '''level=''' Category recursion level, where 0 means unlimited A section-specific GeoGroup makes most sense placed within the section it refers to. == Linking == Links may be made directly to a page's instance of the template, with the ID GeoGroup, by using [[#GeoGroup|Example Link Text]], which produces: [[#GeoGroup|Example Link Text]]. == Expansion == Other KML aware services may be added, as they become available; as might services converting the coordinates to [[GPX]] or [[Point of interest#File formats|other formats]]. == Examples == {| class="wikitable" ! Point ! [[Geographic coordinate system|Coordinates]]
(links to map & photo sources) |- | Dudley Port Junction | {{coord|52.52204|-2.04331|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Dudley Port Junction}} |- | Groveland Bridge (Titpon Road, A457) | {{coord|52.51758|-2.04760|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Groveland Bridge (Titpon Road, A457)}} |- | Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line) | {{coord|52.51640|-2.04843|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line)}} |- | Northern tunnel portal | {{coord|52.51534|-2.04948|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Northern tunnel portal}} |- | Aston Road air vent | {{coord|52.50850|-2.05518|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Aston Road air vent}} |- | Regent Road air vent | {{coord|52.50697|-2.05708|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Regent Road air vent}} |- | Approx. tunnel mid-point | {{coord|52.50435|-2.05932|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Approx. tunnel mid-point}} |- | Southern tunnel portal | {{coord|52.49335|-2.06916|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Southern tunnel portal}} |- | Windmill End Junction | {{coord|52.49157|-2.07019|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Windmill End Junction}} |} == Bookmarklet == To added the template to categories, you may want to use the [[bookmarklet]] below. Sample edit: [http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Category:Loughs_of_County_Mayo&diff=230771016&oldid=165713411] javascript:with(document.forms[0]){void(wpSummary.value+='+{{[[Template:GeoGroup|GeoGroup]]}}%20(see%20also%20[[Wikipedia:WikiProject_Geographical_coordinates|WP:GEO]]');void(wpTextbox1.value='{{GeoGroup}}'+wpTextbox1.value);void(wpSave.click())} [[Category:External link templates|GeoGroup]] [[Category:Mapping templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Geographic coordinate lists]] [[Category:Articles with Geo]] [[bn:টেমপ্লেট:GeoGroupTemplate]] [[ca:Plantilla:Mapa llista coordenades]] [[eo:Ŝablono:GeoGrupo]] [[id:Templat:GeoGroupTemplate]] [[ja:Template:座標一覧]] [[fr:Modèle:GeoGroup]] [[mk:Шаблон:GeoGroupTemplate]] [[no:Mal:Alle koordinater]] [[pl:Szablon:Mapa obiektów]] [[pt:Predefinição:GeoGroupTemplate]] [[sq:Stampa:GeoGroupTemplate]] [[sv:Mall:GeoLänk]]

{{Documentation subpage}} {{tl|GeoGroup}} generates an infobox type list of external links to services that present or display all [[Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] listed on certain Wikipedia articles that have multiples of coordinates. The template is also capable of generating all the title coordinates from many articles listed in a single Wikipedia category. The template is intended to be inserted in the ''External links'' section of articles, or inside a section with a list or table of features, if appropriate (e.g. see [[Tame Valley Canal]]). The linked services allow the following results: * Coordinates from the Wikipedia page or category in a [[Keyhole Markup Language|KML]] file suitable for use in [[Google Earth]], [[NASA World Wind]] and similar applications. * Microformatted coordinates from the Wikipedia page in a [[GeoRSS]] file. * A link to [[Google Maps]] with the waypoints plotted onto the map. Note that Google caches data and may at times show a previous revision instead of the latest one. In this case wait a moment before refreshing. * A link to [[Bing]] map with the waypoints plotted onto the map. Note that if the page or category has more than 200 coordinates, Bing would present a maximum of just the first 200 points. Inline coordinates can be associated with a name using {{Tl|Coord}} with the {{para|name}} parameter, such as: :{{[[Template:Coord|coord]]|1|2|type:landmark|name=Interesting uncharted point in the sea}} However, do not use the {{para|name}} parameter when the coordinates are within a parent template or table which emits a [[Wikipedia:microformats|microformat]], such as an infobox. This template is not intended for articles with single sets of coordinates; such coordinates already link indirectly to {{Tl|GeoTemplate}}. To use this template, add {{tl|GeoGroup}} to the ''External links'' section of an article, or, for list articles with no title coordinate, at the top of the article. == Usage == There are three optional parameters that are useful mainly in articles—not categories: :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=article=''article name''}} '''article=''' The name of article or category, from which coordinates will be displayed. (Can be a category, as shown in [[List of stations in London fare zone 2#List of stations]]) :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=article=''article name''|3=articlee=''article_name''}} '''articlee=''' Encoded name of the article. The encoding process here involves changing spaces to underscores ( '''_''' ), everything else is taken care of by the [[Wikipedia:Magic words#Paths|urlencode magic word]]. :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=section=''section name''}} '''section=''' The section parameter arranges most of the links to use only coordinates in the named section of the article. This is useful to limit the number seen at one time in articles with many coordinates. For examples, see [[List of rapids of the Columbia River]]. There is one optional parameters for use with categories: :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=level=''0,1,2,3,...''}} '''level=''' Category recursion level, where 0 means unlimited A section-specific GeoGroup makes most sense placed within the section it refers to. == Linking == Links may be made directly to a page's instance of the template, with the ID GeoGroup, by using [[#GeoGroup|Example Link Text]], which produces: [[#GeoGroup|Example Link Text]]. == Expansion == Other KML aware services may be added, as they become available; as might services converting the coordinates to [[GPX]] or [[Point of interest#File formats|other formats]]. == Examples == {| class="wikitable" ! Point ! [[Geographic coordinate system|Coordinates]]
(links to map & photo sources) |- | Dudley Port Junction | {{coord|52.52204|-2.04331|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Dudley Port Junction}} |- | Groveland Bridge (Titpon Road, A457) | {{coord|52.51758|-2.04760|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Groveland Bridge (Titpon Road, A457)}} |- | Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line) | {{coord|52.51640|-2.04843|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line)}} |- | Northern tunnel portal | {{coord|52.51534|-2.04948|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Northern tunnel portal}} |- | Aston Road air vent | {{coord|52.50850|-2.05518|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Aston Road air vent}} |- | Regent Road air vent | {{coord|52.50697|-2.05708|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Regent Road air vent}} |- | Approx. tunnel mid-point | {{coord|52.50435|-2.05932|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Approx. tunnel mid-point}} |- | Southern tunnel portal | {{coord|52.49335|-2.06916|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Southern tunnel portal}} |- | Windmill End Junction | {{coord|52.49157|-2.07019|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Windmill End Junction}} |} == Bookmarklet == To added the template to categories, you may want to use the [[bookmarklet]] below. Sample edit: [http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Category:Loughs_of_County_Mayo&diff=230771016&oldid=165713411] javascript:with(document.forms[0]){void(wpSummary.value+='+{{[[Template:GeoGroup|GeoGroup]]}}%20(see%20also%20[[Wikipedia:WikiProject_Geographical_coordinates|WP:GEO]]');void(wpTextbox1.value='{{GeoGroup}}'+wpTextbox1.value);void(wpSave.click())} [[Category:External link templates|GeoGroup]] [[Category:Mapping templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Geographic coordinate lists]] [[Category:Articles with Geo]] [[bn:টেমপ্লেট:GeoGroupTemplate]] [[ca:Plantilla:Mapa llista coordenades]] [[es:Plantilla:GrupoDeCoordenadas]] [[eo:Ŝablono:GeoGrupo]] [[id:Templat:GeoGroupTemplate]] [[ja:Template:座標一覧]] [[fr:Modèle:GeoGroup]] [[mk:Шаблон:GeoGroupTemplate]] [[no:Mal:Alle koordinater]] [[pl:Szablon:Mapa obiektów]] [[pt:Predefinição:GeoGroupTemplate]] [[sq:Stampa:GeoGroupTemplate]] [[sv:Mall:GeoLänk]]

{{Documentation subpage}} {{tl|GeoGroup}} generates an infobox type list of external links to services that present or display all [[Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] listed on certain Wikipedia articles that have multiples of coordinates. The template is also capable of generating all the title coordinates from many articles listed in a single Wikipedia category. The template is intended to be inserted in the ''External links'' section of articles, or inside a section with a list or table of features, if appropriate (e.g. see [[Tame Valley Canal]]). The linked services allow the following results: * Coordinates from the Wikipedia page or category in a [[Keyhole Markup Language|KML]] file suitable for use in [[Google Earth]], [[NASA World Wind]] and similar applications. * Microformatted coordinates from the Wikipedia page in a [[GeoRSS]] file. * A link to [[Google Maps]] with the waypoints plotted onto the map. Note that Google caches data and may at times show a previous revision instead of the latest one. In this case wait a moment before refreshing. * A link to [[Bing]] map with the waypoints plotted onto the map. Note that if the page or category has more than 200 coordinates, Bing would present a maximum of just the first 200 points. Inline coordinates can be associated with a name using {{Tl|Coord}} with the {{para|name}} parameter, such as: :{{[[Template:Coord|coord]]|1|2|type:landmark|name=Interesting uncharted point in the sea}} However, do not use the {{para|name}} parameter when the coordinates are within a parent template or table which emits a [[Wikipedia:microformats|microformat]], such as an infobox. This template is not intended for articles with single sets of coordinates; such coordinates already link indirectly to {{Tl|GeoTemplate}}. To use this template, add {{tl|GeoGroup}} to the ''External links'' section of an article, or, for list articles with no title coordinate, at the top of the article. == Usage == There are three optional parameters that are useful mainly in articles—not categories: :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=article=''article name''}} '''article=''' The name of article or category, from which coordinates will be displayed. (Can be a category, as shown in [[List of stations in London fare zone 2#List of stations]]) :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=article=''article name''|3=articlee=''article_name''}} '''articlee=''' Encoded name of the article. The encoding process here involves changing spaces to underscores ( '''_''' ), everything else is taken care of by the [[Wikipedia:Magic words#Paths|urlencode magic word]]. :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=section=''section name''}} '''section=''' The section parameter arranges most of the links to use only coordinates in the named section of the article. This is useful to limit the number seen at one time in articles with many coordinates. For examples, see [[List of rapids of the Columbia River]]. There is one optional parameters for use with categories: :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=level=''0,1,2,3,...''}} '''level=''' Category recursion level, where 0 means unlimited A section-specific GeoGroup makes most sense placed within the section it refers to. ==Cache delay== As pages are [[Web cache|cached]] at Google and Bing, it is normal for the new template not to appear for several hours. Its appearance can be forced by altering the [[url]] on the browser to On the template click on the Google map link. Up comes a map with error message- go to the browser searchurl line (top left) change the address to: : ''https://maps.google.com/maps?q=http://toolserver.org/~para/cgi-bin/kmlexport%3Farticle%3DCurrent_article_name%26usecache%3D0'' from ''....re%26usecache%3D1''. That is: the last character changes from 1 to zero. If it is in plain text try: :Putting ''usecache=0'' instead of the ''usecache=1''. == Linking == Links may be made directly to a page's instance of the template, with the ID GeoGroup, by using [[#GeoGroup|Example Link Text]], which produces: [[#GeoGroup|Example Link Text]]. == Expansion == Other KML aware services may be added, as they become available; as might services converting the coordinates to [[GPX]] or [[Point of interest#File formats|other formats]]. == Examples == {| class="wikitable" ! Point ! [[Geographic coordinate system|Coordinates]]
(links to map & photo sources) |- | Dudley Port Junction | {{coord|52.52204|-2.04331|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Dudley Port Junction}} |- | Groveland Bridge (Titpon Road, A457) | {{coord|52.51758|-2.04760|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Groveland Bridge (Titpon Road, A457)}} |- | Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line) | {{coord|52.51640|-2.04843|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line)}} |- | Northern tunnel portal | {{coord|52.51534|-2.04948|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Northern tunnel portal}} |- | Aston Road air vent | {{coord|52.50850|-2.05518|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Aston Road air vent}} |- | Regent Road air vent | {{coord|52.50697|-2.05708|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Regent Road air vent}} |- | Approx. tunnel mid-point | {{coord|52.50435|-2.05932|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Approx. tunnel mid-point}} |- | Southern tunnel portal | {{coord|52.49335|-2.06916|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Southern tunnel portal}} |- | Windmill End Junction | {{coord|52.49157|-2.07019|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Windmill End Junction}} |} == Bookmarklet == To added the template to categories, you may want to use the [[bookmarklet]] below.
