{{Documentation subpage}} {{tl|GeoGroup}} generates an infobox type list of external links to services that present or display all [[Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] listed on certain Wikipedia articles that have multiples of coordinates. The template is also capable of generating all the title coordinates from many articles listed in a single Wikipedia category. The template is intended to be inserted in the ''External links'' section of articles, or inside a section with a list or table of features, if appropriate (e.g. see [[Tame Valley Canal]]). The linked services allow the following results: * Coordinates from the Wikipedia page or category in a [[Keyhole Markup Language|KML]] file suitable for use in [[Google Earth]], [[NASA World Wind]] and similar applications. * Microformatted coordinates from the Wikipedia page in a [[GeoRSS]] file. * A link to [[Google Maps]] with the waypoints plotted onto the map. Note that Google caches data and may at times show a previous revision instead of the latest one. In this case wait a moment before refreshing. * A link to [[Bing]] map with the waypoints plotted onto the map. Note that if the page or category has more than 200 coordinates, Bing would present a maximum of just the first 200 points. Inline coordinates can be associated with a name using {{Tl|Coord}} with the {{para|name}} parameter, such as: :{{[[Template:Coord|coord]]|1|2|type:landmark|name=Interesting uncharted point in the sea}} However, do not use the {{para|name}} parameter when the coordinates are within a parent template or table which emits a [[Wikipedia:microformats|microformat]], such as an infobox. This template is not intended for articles with single sets of coordinates; such coordinates already link indirectly to {{Tl|GeoTemplate}}. To use this template, add {{tl|GeoGroup}} to the ''External links'' section of an article, or, for list articles with no title coordinate, at the top of the article. == Usage == There are three optional parameters that are useful mainly in articles—not categories: :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=article=''article name''}} '''article=''' The name of article or category, from which coordinates will be displayed. (Can be a category, as shown in [[List of stations in London fare zone 2#List of stations]]) :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=article=''article name''|3=articlee=''article_name''}} '''articlee=''' Encoded name of the article. The encoding process here involves changing spaces to underscores ( '''_''' ), everything else is taken care of by the [[Wikipedia:Magic words#Paths|urlencode magic word]]. :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=section=''section name''}} '''section=''' The section parameter arranges most of the links to use only coordinates in the named section of the article. This is useful to limit the number seen at one time in articles with many coordinates. For examples, see [[List of rapids of the Columbia River]]. There is one optional parameters for use with categories: :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=level=''0,1,2,3,...''}} '''level=''' Category recursion level, where 0 means unlimited A section-specific GeoGroup makes most sense placed within the section it refers to. ==Cache delay== As pages are [[Web cache|cached]] at Google and Bing, it is normal for the new template not to appear for several hours. Its appearance can be forced by altering the [[url]] on the browser. On the template click on the Google map link. Up comes an old map or one with error message. Go to the browser ''searchurl line'' (top left) change the address to: : ''https://maps.google.com/maps?q=http://toolserver.org/~para/cgi-bin/kmlexport%3Farticle%3DCurrent_article_name%26usecache%3D0'' from ''....%26usecache%3D1''. That is: the last character changes from 1 to zero. If it is in plain text try: :Putting ''usecache=0'' instead of the ''usecache=1''. == Linking == Links may be made directly to a page's instance of the template, with the ID GeoGroup, by using [[#GeoGroup|Example Link Text]], which produces: [[#GeoGroup|Example Link Text]]. == Expansion == Other KML aware services may be added, as they become available; as might services converting the coordinates to [[GPX]] or [[Point of interest#File formats|other formats]]. == Examples == {| class="wikitable" ! Point ! [[Geographic coordinate system|Coordinates]]

Sample edit: [http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Category:Loughs_of_County_Mayo&diff=230771016&oldid=165713411] javascript:with(document.forms[0]){void(wpSummary.value+='+{{[[Template:GeoGroup|GeoGroup]]}}%20(see%20also%20[[Wikipedia:WikiProject_Geographical_coordinates|WP:GEO]]');void(wpTextbox1.value='{{GeoGroup}}'+wpTextbox1.value);void(wpSave.click())} [[Category:External link templates|GeoGroup]] [[Category:Mapping templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Geographic coordinate lists]] [[Category:Articles with Geo]] [[bn:টেমপ্লেট:GeoGroupTemplate]] [[ca:Plantilla:Mapa llista coordenades]] [[es:Plantilla:GrupoDeCoordenadas]] [[eo:Ŝablono:GeoGrupo]] [[id:Templat:GeoGroupTemplate]] [[ja:Template:座標一覧]] [[fr:Modèle:GeoGroup]] [[mk:Шаблон:GeoGroupTemplate]] [[no:Mal:Alle koordinater]] [[pl:Szablon:Mapa obiektów]] [[pt:Predefinição:GeoGroupTemplate]] [[sq:Stampa:GeoGroupTemplate]] [[sv:Mall:GeoLänk]]

Sample edit: [http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Category:Loughs_of_County_Mayo&diff=230771016&oldid=165713411] javascript:with(document.forms[0]){void(wpSummary.value+='+{{[[Template:GeoGroup|GeoGroup]]}}%20(see%20also%20[[Wikipedia:WikiProject_Geographical_coordinates|WP:GEO]]');void(wpTextbox1.value='{{GeoGroup}}'+wpTextbox1.value);void(wpSave.click())} [[Category:External link templates|GeoGroup]] [[Category:Mapping templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Geographic coordinate lists]] [[Category:Articles with Geo]] [[bn:টেমপ্লেট:GeoGroupTemplate]] [[ca:Plantilla:Mapa llista coordenades]] [[es:Plantilla:GrupoDeCoordenadas]] [[eo:Ŝablono:GeoGrupo]] [[id:Templat:GeoGroupTemplate]] [[ja:Template:座標一覧]] [[fr:Modèle:GeoGroup]] [[mk:Шаблон:GeoGroupTemplate]] [[no:Mal:Alle koordinater]] [[pl:Szablon:Mapa obiektów]] [[pt:Predefinição:GeoGroupTemplate]] [[sq:Stampa:GeoGroupTemplate]] [[sv:Mall:GeoLänk]] [[zh:Template:GeoGroupTemplate]]

{{Documentation subpage}} {{tl|GeoGroup}} generates an infobox type list of external links to services that present or display all [[Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] listed on certain Wikipedia articles that have multiples of coordinates. The template is also capable of generating all the title coordinates from many articles listed in a single Wikipedia category. The template is intended to be inserted in the ''External links'' section of articles, or inside a section with a list or table of features, if appropriate (e.g. see [[Tame Valley Canal]]). The linked services allow the following results: * Coordinates from the Wikipedia page or category in a [[Keyhole Markup Language|KML]] file suitable for use in [[Google Earth]], [[NASA World Wind]] and similar applications. * Microformatted coordinates from the Wikipedia page in a [[GeoRSS]] file. * A link to [[Google Maps]] with the waypoints plotted onto the map. Note that Google caches data and may at times show a previous revision instead of the latest one. In this case wait a moment before refreshing. * A link to [[Bing]] map with the waypoints plotted onto the map. Note that if the page or category has more than 200 coordinates, Bing would present a maximum of just the first 200 points. Inline coordinates can be associated with a name using {{Tl|Coord}} with the {{para|name}} parameter, such as: :{{[[Template:Coord|coord]]|1|2|type:landmark|name=Interesting uncharted point in the sea}} However, do not use the {{para|name}} parameter when the coordinates are within a parent template or table which emits a [[Wikipedia:microformats|microformat]], such as an infobox. This template is not intended for articles with single sets of coordinates; such coordinates already link indirectly to {{Tl|GeoTemplate}}. To use this template, add {{tl|GeoGroup}} to the ''External links'' section of an article, or, for list articles with no title coordinate, at the top of the article. == Usage == There are three optional parameters that are useful mainly in articles—not categories: :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=article=''article name''}} '''article=''' The name of article or category, from which coordinates will be displayed. (Can be a category, as shown in [[List of stations in London fare zone 2#List of stations]]) :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=article=''article name''|3=articlee=''article_name''}} '''articlee=''' Encoded name of the article. The encoding process here involves changing spaces to underscores ( '''_''' ), everything else is taken care of by the [[Wikipedia:Magic words#Paths|urlencode magic word]]. :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=section=''section name''}} '''section=''' The section parameter arranges most of the links to use only coordinates in the named section of the article. This is useful to limit the number seen at one time in articles with many coordinates. For examples, see [[List of rapids of the Columbia River]]. There is one optional parameters for use with categories: :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=level=''0,1,2,3,...''}} '''level=''' Category recursion level, where 0 means unlimited A section-specific GeoGroup makes most sense placed within the section it refers to. ==Cache delay== As pages are [[Web cache|cached]] at Google and Bing, it is normal for the new template not to appear for several hours. Its appearance can be forced by altering the [[url]] on the browser. On the template click on the Google map link. Up comes an old map or one with error message. Go to the browser ''searchurl line'' (top left) change the address to: : ''https://maps.google.com/maps?q=http://toolserver.org/~para/cgi-bin/kmlexport%3Farticle%3DCurrent_article_name%26usecache%3D0'' from ''....%26usecache%3D1''. That is: the last character changes from 1 to zero. If it is in plain text try: :Putting ''usecache=0'' instead of the ''usecache=1''. == Linking == Links may be made directly to a page's instance of the template, with the ID GeoGroup, by using [[#GeoGroup|Example Link Text]], which produces: [[#GeoGroup|Example Link Text]]. == Expansion == Other KML aware services may be added, as they become available; as might services converting the coordinates to [[GPX]] or [[Point of interest#File formats|other formats]]. == Examples == {| class="wikitable" ! Point ! [[Geographic coordinate system|Coordinates]]
(links to map & photo sources) |- | Dudley Port Junction | {{coord|52.52204|-2.04331|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Dudley Port Junction}} |- | Groveland Bridge (Titpon Road, A457) | {{coord|52.51758|-2.04760|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Groveland Bridge (Titpon Road, A457)}} |- | Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line) | {{coord|52.51640|-2.04843|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line)}} |- | Northern tunnel portal | {{coord|52.51534|-2.04948|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Northern tunnel portal}} |- | Aston Road air vent | {{coord|52.50850|-2.05518|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Aston Road air vent}} |- | Regent Road air vent | {{coord|52.50697|-2.05708|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Regent Road air vent}} |- | Approx. tunnel mid-point | {{coord|52.50435|-2.05932|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Approx. tunnel mid-point}} |- | Southern tunnel portal | {{coord|52.49335|-2.06916|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Southern tunnel portal}} |- | Windmill End Junction | {{coord|52.49157|-2.07019|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Windmill End Junction}} |} == Bookmarklet == To added the template to categories, you may want to use the [[bookmarklet]] below. Sample edit: [http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Category:Loughs_of_County_Mayo&diff=230771016&oldid=165713411] javascript:with(document.forms[0]){void(wpSummary.value+='+{{[[Template:GeoGroup|GeoGroup]]}}%20(see%20also%20[[Wikipedia:WikiProject_Geographical_coordinates|WP:GEO]]');void(wpTextbox1.value='{{GeoGroup}}'+wpTextbox1.value);void(wpSave.click())} [[Category:External link templates|GeoGroup]] [[Category:Mapping templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Geographic coordinate lists]] [[Category:Articles with Geo]] [[bn:টেমপ্লেট:GeoGroupTemplate]] [[ca:Plantilla:Mapa llista coordenades]] [[es:Plantilla:GrupoDeCoordenadas]] [[eo:Ŝablono:GeoGrupo]] [[id:Templat:GeoGroupTemplate]] [[ja:Template:座標一覧]] [[fr:Modèle:GeoGroup]] [[mk:Шаблон:GeoGroupTemplate]] [[no:Mal:Alle koordinater]] [[pl:Szablon:Mapa obiektów]] [[pt:Predefinição:GeoGroupTemplate]] [[sq:Stampa:GeoGroupTemplate]] [[sv:Mall:GeoLänk]] [[zh:Template:GeoGroupTemplate]]

In this case wait a moment before refreshing. * A link to [[Bing]] map with the waypoints plotted onto the map. Note that if the page or category has more than 200 coordinates, Bing would present a maximum of just the first 200 points. Inline coordinates can be associated with a name using {{Tl|Coord}} with the {{para|name}} parameter, such as: :{{[[Template:Coord|coord]]|1|2|type:landmark|name=Interesting uncharted point in the sea}} However, do not use the {{para|name}} parameter when the coordinates are within a parent template or table which emits a [[Wikipedia:microformats|microformat]], such as an infobox. This template is not intended for articles with single sets of coordinates; such coordinates already link indirectly to {{Tl|GeoTemplate}}. To use this template, add {{tl|GeoGroup}} to the ''External links'' section of an article, or, for list articles with no title coordinate, at the top of the article. == Usage == There are three optional parameters that are useful mainly in articles—not categories: :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=article=''article name''}} '''article=''' The name of article or category, from which coordinates will be displayed. (Can be a category, as shown in [[List of stations in London fare zone 2#List of stations]]) :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=article=''article name''|3=articlee=''article_name''}} '''articlee=''' Encoded name of the article. The encoding process here involves changing spaces to underscores ( '''_''' ), everything else is taken care of by the [[Wikipedia:Magic words#Paths|urlencode magic word]]. :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=section=''section name''}} '''section=''' The section parameter arranges most of the links to use only coordinates in the named section of the article. This is useful to limit the number seen at one time in articles with many coordinates. For examples, see [[List of rapids of the Columbia River]]. There is one optional parameter for use with categories: :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=level=''0,1,2,3,...''}} '''level=''' Category recursion level, where 0 means unlimited A section-specific GeoGroup makes most sense placed within the section it refers to. == Cache delay == As pages are [[Web cache|cached]] at Google and Bing, it is normal for the new template not to appear for several hours. Its appearance can be forced by altering the [[url]] on the browser. On the template click on the Google map link. Up comes an old map or one with error message. Go to the browser ''searchurl line'' (top left) change the address to: : ''https://maps.google.com/maps?q=http://toolserver.org/~para/cgi-bin/kmlexport%3Farticle%3DCurrent_article_name%26usecache%3D0'' from ''....%26usecache%3D1''. That is: the last character changes from 1 to zero. If it is in plain text try: :Putting ''usecache=0'' instead of the ''usecache=1''. == Linking == Links may be made directly to a page's instance of the template, with the ID GeoGroup, by using [[#GeoGroup|Example Link Text]], which produces: [[#GeoGroup|Example Link Text]]. == Expansion == Other KML aware services may be added, as they become available; as might services converting the coordinates to [[GPX]] or [[Point of interest#File formats|other formats]]. == Examples == {{GeoGroup|section=Examples}} {| class="wikitable" ! Point ! [[Geographic coordinate system|Coordinates]]
(links to map & photo sources) |- | Dudley Port Junction | {{coord|52.52204|-2.04331|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Dudley Port Junction}} |- | Groveland Bridge (Tipton Road, A457) | {{coord|52.51758|-2.04760|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Groveland Bridge (Tipton Road, A457)}} |- | Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line) | {{coord|52.51640|-2.04843|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line)}} |- | Northern tunnel portal | {{coord|52.51534|-2.04948|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Northern tunnel portal}} |- | Aston Road air vent | {{coord|52.50850|-2.05518|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Aston Road air vent}} |- | Regent Road air vent | {{coord|52.50697|-2.05708|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Regent Road air vent}} |- | Approx. tunnel mid-point | {{coord|52.50435|-2.05932|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Approx. tunnel mid-point}} |- | Southern tunnel portal | {{coord|52.49335|-2.06916|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Southern tunnel portal}} |- | Windmill End Junction | {{coord|52.49157|-2.07019|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Windmill End Junction}} |} == Bookmarklet == To added the template to categories, you may want to use the [[bookmarklet]] below. Sample edit: [http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Category:Loughs_of_County_Mayo&diff=230771016&oldid=165713411] javascript:with(document.forms[0]){void(wpSummary.value+='+{{[[Template:GeoGroup|GeoGroup]]}}%20(see%20also%20[[Wikipedia:WikiProject_Geographical_coordinates|WP:GEO]]');void(wpTextbox1.value='{{GeoGroup}}'+wpTextbox1.value);void(wpSave.click())} {{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|sandbox | | [[Category:External link templates]] [[Category:Map formatting and function templates]] [[Category:Geographic coordinate lists|τ]] [[Category:Articles with Geo|τ]] }}

revision.comment: revise possible results list into order as presented by the template, and clarify (hope this is correct, please check & fix if i am wrong) that 5 options each would export a file.
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{tl|GeoGroup}} generates an infobox type list of external links to services that present or display all [[Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] listed on certain Wikipedia articles that have multiples of coordinates. The template is also capable of generating all the title coordinates from many articles listed in a single Wikipedia category. The template is intended to be inserted in the ''External links'' section of articles, or inside a section with a list or table of features, if appropriate (e.g. see [[Tame Valley Canal]]). The linked services allow the following results: * A link to [[Bing]] map with the waypoints plotted onto the map. Note that if the page or category has more than 200 coordinates, not all will be plotted. Bing will present just the first 200 points. * Export all coordinates from the Wikipedia page or category in a [[Keyhole Markup Language|KML]] file suitable for use in [[Google Earth]], [[NASA World Wind]] and similar applications. * Export all coordinates in a GeoRSS file. * Export all coordinates in a GPX file. * Export Microformatted coordinates from the Wikipedia page in a [[GeoRSS]] file. * Export place data in an RDF file. Inline coordinates can be associated with a name using {{Tl|Coord}} with the {{para|name}} parameter, such as: :{{[[Template:Coord|coord]]|1|2|type:landmark|name=Interesting uncharted point in the sea}} However, do not use the {{para|name}} parameter when the coordinates are within a parent template or table which emits a [[Wikipedia:microformats|microformat]], such as an infobox. This template is not intended for articles with single sets of coordinates; such coordinates already link indirectly to {{Tl|GeoTemplate}}. To use this template, add {{tl|GeoGroup}} to the ''External links'' section of an article, or, for list articles with no title coordinate, at the top of the article. == Usage == There are three optional parameters that are useful mainly in articles—not categories: :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=article=''article name''}} '''article=''' The name of article or category, from which coordinates will be displayed. (Can be a category, as shown in [[List of stations in London fare zone 2#List of stations]]) :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=article=''article name''|3=articlee=''article_name''}} '''articlee=''' Encoded name of the article. The encoding process here involves changing spaces to underscores ( '''_''' ), everything else is taken care of by the [[Wikipedia:Magic words#Paths|urlencode magic word]]. :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=section=''section name''}} '''section=''' The section parameter arranges most of the links to use only coordinates in the named section of the article. This is useful to limit the number seen at one time in articles with many coordinates. For examples, see [[List of rapids of the Columbia River]]. There is one optional parameter for use with categories: :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=level=''0,1,2,3,...''}} '''level=''' Category recursion level, where 0 means unlimited A section-specific GeoGroup makes most sense placed within the section it refers to. == Cache delay == As pages are [[Web cache|cached]] at Google and Bing, it is normal for the new template not to appear for several hours. Its appearance can be forced by altering the [[url]] on the browser. On the template click on the Google map link. Up comes an old map or one with error message. Go to the browser ''searchurl line'' (top left) change the address to: : ''https://maps.google.com/maps?q=http://toolserver.org/~para/cgi-bin/kmlexport%3Farticle%3DCurrent_article_name%26usecache%3D0'' from ''....%26usecache%3D1''. That is: the last character changes from 1 to zero. If it is in plain text try: :Putting ''usecache=0'' instead of the ''usecache=1''. == Linking == Links may be made directly to a page's instance of the template, with the ID GeoGroup, by using [[#GeoGroup|Example Link Text]], which produces: [[#GeoGroup|Example Link Text]]. == Expansion == Other KML aware services may be added, as they become available; as might services converting the coordinates to [[GPX]] or [[Point of interest#File formats|other formats]]. == Examples == {{GeoGroup{{\sandbox}}|section=Examples}} {| class="wikitable" ! Point ! [[Geographic coordinate system|Coordinates]]

revision.comment: insert mention of Open Street Map plotting now available...which is great...thanks to whomever is responsible!
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{tl|GeoGroup}} generates an infobox type list of external links to services that present or display all [[Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] listed on certain Wikipedia articles that have multiples of coordinates. The template is also capable of generating all the title coordinates from many articles listed in a single Wikipedia category. The template is intended to be inserted in the ''External links'' section of articles, or inside a section with a list or table of features, if appropriate (e.g. see [[Tame Valley Canal]]). The linked services allow the following results: * A link to an [[Open Street Map]] map with the waypoints plotted onto the map. * A link to [[Bing]] map with the waypoints plotted onto the map. Note that if the page or category has more than 200 coordinates, not all will be plotted. Bing will present just the first 200 points. * Export all coordinates from the Wikipedia page or category in a [[Keyhole Markup Language|KML]] file suitable for use in [[Google Earth]], [[NASA World Wind]] and similar applications. * Export all coordinates in a GeoRSS file. * Export all coordinates in a GPX file. * Export Microformatted coordinates from the Wikipedia page in a [[GeoRSS]] file. * Export place data in an RDF file. Inline coordinates can be associated with a name using {{Tl|Coord}} with the {{para|name}} parameter, such as: :{{[[Template:Coord|coord]]|1|2|type:landmark|name=Interesting uncharted point in the sea}} However, do not use the {{para|name}} parameter when the coordinates are within a parent template or table which emits a [[Wikipedia:microformats|microformat]], such as an infobox. This template is not intended for articles with single sets of coordinates; such coordinates already link indirectly to {{Tl|GeoTemplate}}. To use this template, add {{tl|GeoGroup}} to the ''External links'' section of an article, or, for list articles with no title coordinate, at the top of the article. == Usage == There are three optional parameters that are useful mainly in articles—not categories: :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=article=''article name''}} '''article=''' The name of article or category, from which coordinates will be displayed. (Can be a category, as shown in [[List of stations in London fare zone 2#List of stations]]) :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=article=''article name''|3=articlee=''article_name''}} '''articlee=''' Encoded name of the article. The encoding process here involves changing spaces to underscores ( '''_''' ), everything else is taken care of by the [[Wikipedia:Magic words#Paths|urlencode magic word]]. :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=section=''section name''}} '''section=''' The section parameter arranges most of the links to use only coordinates in the named section of the article. This is useful to limit the number seen at one time in articles with many coordinates. For examples, see [[List of rapids of the Columbia River]]. There is one optional parameter for use with categories: :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=level=''0,1,2,3,...''}} '''level=''' Category recursion level, where 0 means unlimited A section-specific GeoGroup makes most sense placed within the section it refers to. == Cache delay == As pages are [[Web cache|cached]] at Google and Bing, it is normal for the new template not to appear for several hours. Its appearance can be forced by altering the [[url]] on the browser. On the template click on the Google map link. Up comes an old map or one with error message. Go to the browser ''searchurl line'' (top left) change the address to: : ''https://maps.google.com/maps?q=http://toolserver.org/~para/cgi-bin/kmlexport%3Farticle%3DCurrent_article_name%26usecache%3D0'' from ''....%26usecache%3D1''. That is: the last character changes from 1 to zero. If it is in plain text try: :Putting ''usecache=0'' instead of the ''usecache=1''. == Linking == Links may be made directly to a page's instance of the template, with the ID GeoGroup, by using [[#GeoGroup|Example Link Text]], which produces: [[#GeoGroup|Example Link Text]]. == Expansion == Other KML aware services may be added, as they become available; as might services converting the coordinates to [[GPX]] or [[Point of interest#File formats|other formats]]. == Examples == {{GeoGroup{{\sandbox}}|section=Examples}} {| class="wikitable" ! Point ! [[Geographic coordinate system|Coordinates]]
(links to map & photo sources) |- | Dudley Port Junction | {{coord|52.52204|-2.04331|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Dudley Port Junction}} |- | Groveland Bridge (Tipton Road, A457) | {{coord|52.51758|-2.04760|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Groveland Bridge (Tipton Road, A457)}} |- | Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line) | {{coord|52.51640|-2.04843|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line)}} |- | Northern tunnel portal | {{coord|52.51534|-2.04948|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Northern tunnel portal}} |- | Aston Road air vent | {{coord|52.50850|-2.05518|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Aston Road air vent}} |- | Regent Road air vent | {{coord|52.50697|-2.05708|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Regent Road air vent}} |- | Approx. tunnel mid-point | {{coord|52.50435|-2.05932|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Approx. tunnel mid-point}} |- | Southern tunnel portal | {{coord|52.49335|-2.06916|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Southern tunnel portal}} |- | Windmill End Junction | {{coord|52.49157|-2.07019|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Windmill End Junction}} |} == Bookmarklet == To added the template to categories, you may want to use the [[bookmarklet]] below. Sample edit: [http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Category:Loughs_of_County_Mayo&diff=230771016&oldid=165713411] javascript:with(document.forms[0]){void(wpSummary.value+='+{{[[Template:GeoGroup|GeoGroup]]}}%20(see%20also%20[[Wikipedia:WikiProject_Geographical_coordinates|WP:GEO]]');void(wpTextbox1.value='{{GeoGroup}}'+wpTextbox1.value);void(wpSave.click())} {{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|sandbox | | [[Category:External link templates]] [[Category:Map formatting and function templates]] [[Category:Geographic coordinate lists|τ]] [[Category:Articles with Geo|τ]] }}

revision.comment: /* Cache delay */ retitle to /* Updating maps / cache delay */ and rewrite for clarity plus to provide updated instructions for OSM and Bing
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{tl|GeoGroup}} generates an infobox type list of external links to services that present or display all [[Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] listed on certain Wikipedia articles that have multiples of coordinates. The template is also capable of generating all the title coordinates from many articles listed in a single Wikipedia category. The template is intended to be inserted in the ''External links'' section of articles, or inside a section with a list or table of features, if appropriate (e.g. see [[Tame Valley Canal]]). The linked services allow the following results: * A link to an [[Open Street Map]] map with the waypoints plotted onto the map. * A link to [[Bing]] map with the waypoints plotted onto the map. Note that if the page or category has more than 200 coordinates, not all will be plotted. Bing will present just the first 200 points. * Export all coordinates from the Wikipedia page or category in a [[Keyhole Markup Language|KML]] file suitable for use in [[Google Earth]], [[NASA World Wind]] and similar applications. * Export all coordinates in a GeoRSS file. * Export all coordinates in a GPX file. * Export Microformatted coordinates from the Wikipedia page in a [[GeoRSS]] file. * Export place data in an RDF file. Inline coordinates can be associated with a name using {{Tl|Coord}} with the {{para|name}} parameter, such as: :{{[[Template:Coord|coord]]|1|2|type:landmark|name=Interesting uncharted point in the sea}} However, do not use the {{para|name}} parameter when the coordinates are within a parent template or table which emits a [[Wikipedia:microformats|microformat]], such as an infobox. This template is not intended for articles with single sets of coordinates; such coordinates already link indirectly to {{Tl|GeoTemplate}}. To use this template, add {{tl|GeoGroup}} to the ''External links'' section of an article, or, for list articles with no title coordinate, at the top of the article. == Usage == There are three optional parameters that are useful mainly in articles—not categories: :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=article=''article name''}} '''article=''' The name of article or category, from which coordinates will be displayed. (Can be a category, as shown in [[List of stations in London fare zone 2#List of stations]]) :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=article=''article name''|3=articlee=''article_name''}} '''articlee=''' Encoded name of the article. The encoding process here involves changing spaces to underscores ( '''_''' ), everything else is taken care of by the [[Wikipedia:Magic words#Paths|urlencode magic word]]. :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=section=''section name''}} '''section=''' The section parameter arranges most of the links to use only coordinates in the named section of the article. This is useful to limit the number seen at one time in articles with many coordinates. For examples, see [[List of rapids of the Columbia River]]. There is one optional parameter for use with categories: :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=level=''0,1,2,3,...''}} '''level=''' Category recursion level, where 0 means unlimited A section-specific GeoGroup makes most sense placed within the section it refers to. == Updating maps / cache delay == Currently (as of 18 March 2015) maps displayed on Open Street Map are regenerated with each access, and no [[Web cache|caching]] is used. So if an editor adds or changes coordinates in an article, then clicks on the GeoGroup template to "Map all coordinates using OSM", the OSM map displayed will have been updated. For Bing maps (and previously for Google maps which were also included in GeoGroup), pages are [[Web cache|cached]] and new visits to a map corresponding to a Wikipedia article will, by default, continue to use any already-cached data (perhaps from any user's recent visit to the map). The cache may not be cleared for several hours, and only then would the updated map appear. Updating can be forced by altering the [[url]] in the browser to override the Bing's default usage of cached data. When a Bing map is displayed, the URL shown in your browser's "searchurl line" (usually at top left of your browser window), may show something like: ::''http://www.bing.com/maps/?mapurl=http%3A%2F%2Ftools.wmflabs.org%2Fkmlexport%2F%3Farticle%3DCurrent_article_name%26usecache%3D1'' Use your backspace or delete key to erase the "use cache" default choice at the end, so what appears is: ::''http://www.bing.com/maps/?mapurl=http%3A%2F%2Ftools.wmflabs.org%2Fkmlexport%2F%3Farticle%3DCurrent_article_name'' Then hit return and an updated map will be displayed. Or, simply change the last character "1" to "0", so what appears is: ::''http://www.bing.com/maps/?mapurl=http%3A%2F%2Ftools.wmflabs.org%2Fkmlexport%2F%3Farticle%3DCurrent_article_name%26usecache%3D0'' Or if what is displayed for URL is in plain text try: :Replace ''usecache=1'' by ''usecache=0'' instead. == Linking == Links may be made directly to a page's instance of the template, with the ID GeoGroup, by using [[#GeoGroup|Example Link Text]], which produces: [[#GeoGroup|Example Link Text]]. == Expansion == Other KML aware services may be added, as they become available; as might services converting the coordinates to [[GPX]] or [[Point of interest#File formats|other formats]]. == Examples == {{GeoGroup{{\sandbox}}|section=Examples}} {| class="wikitable" ! Point ! [[Geographic coordinate system|Coordinates]]
(links to map & photo sources) |- | Dudley Port Junction | {{coord|52.52204|-2.04331|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Dudley Port Junction}} |- | Groveland Bridge (Tipton Road, A457) | {{coord|52.51758|-2.04760|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Groveland Bridge (Tipton Road, A457)}} |- | Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line) | {{coord|52.51640|-2.04843|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line)}} |- | Northern tunnel portal | {{coord|52.51534|-2.04948|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Northern tunnel portal}} |- | Aston Road air vent | {{coord|52.50850|-2.05518|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Aston Road air vent}} |- | Regent Road air vent | {{coord|52.50697|-2.05708|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Regent Road air vent}} |- | Approx. tunnel mid-point | {{coord|52.50435|-2.05932|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Approx. tunnel mid-point}} |- | Southern tunnel portal | {{coord|52.49335|-2.06916|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Southern tunnel portal}} |- | Windmill End Junction | {{coord|52.49157|-2.07019|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Windmill End Junction}} |} == Bookmarklet == To added the template to categories, you may want to use the [[bookmarklet]] below. Sample edit: [http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Category:Loughs_of_County_Mayo&diff=230771016&oldid=165713411] javascript:with(document.forms[0]){void(wpSummary.value+='+{{[[Template:GeoGroup|GeoGroup]]}}%20(see%20also%20[[Wikipedia:WikiProject_Geographical_coordinates|WP:GEO]]');void(wpTextbox1.value='{{GeoGroup}}'+wpTextbox1.value);void(wpSave.click())} {{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|sandbox | | [[Category:External link templates]] [[Category:Map formatting and function templates]] [[Category:Geographic coordinate lists|τ]] [[Category:Articles with Geo|τ]] }}

Note that if the page or category has more than 200 coordinates, not all will be plotted. Bing will present just the first 200 points. * Export all coordinates from the Wikipedia page or category in a [[Keyhole Markup Language|KML]] file suitable for use in [[Google Earth]], [[NASA World Wind]] and similar applications. * Export all coordinates in a GeoRSS file. * Export all coordinates in a GPX file. * Export Microformatted coordinates from the Wikipedia page in a [[GeoRSS]] file. * Export place data in an RDF file. {{TOCright}} ==Usage== Inline coordinates can be associated with a name using {{Tl|Coord}} with the {{para|name}} parameter, such as: :{{[[Template:Coord|coord]]|1|2|type:landmark|name=Interesting uncharted point in the sea}} However, do not use the {{para|name}} parameter when the coordinates are within a parent template or table which emits a [[Wikipedia:microformats|microformat]], such as an infobox. This template is not intended for articles with single sets of coordinates; such coordinates already link indirectly to {{Tl|GeoTemplate}}. To use this template, add {{tl|GeoGroup}} to the ''External links'' section of an article, or, for list articles with no title coordinate, at the top of the article. === Options for articles and sections === There are three optional parameters that are useful mainly to display coordinates in articles or sections of articles—not categories: :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=article=''article name''}} '''article=''' The name of article or category, from which coordinates will be displayed. (Can be a category, as shown in [[List of stations in London fare zone 2#List of stations]]) :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=article=''article name''|3=articlee=''article_name''}} '''articlee=''' Encoded name of the article. The encoding process here involves changing spaces to underscores ( '''_''' ), everything else is taken care of by the [[Wikipedia:Magic words#Paths|urlencode magic word]]. :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=section=''section name''}} '''section=''' The section parameter arranges most of the links to use only coordinates in the named section of the article. So if an editor adds or changes coordinates in an article, then clicks on the GeoGroup template to "Map all coordinates using OSM", the OSM map displayed will have been updated. For Bing maps (and previously for Google maps which were also included in GeoGroup), pages are [[Web cache|cached]]. New visits to a map corresponding to a Wikipedia article will, by default, continue to use any already-cached data (that has been stored from your last visit, or perhaps from some other Wikipedia user's recent visit to the map). The cache may not be cleared for several hours, and only then would a visit yield an updated map. Updating can be forced easily, however. Simply alter the [[url]] in the browser to override the Bing's default usage of cached data. Or if what is displayed for URL is in plain text try: :Replace ''usecache=1'' by ''usecache=0'' instead. == Linking == Links may be made directly to a page's instance of the template, with the ID GeoGroup, by using [[#GeoGroup|Example Link Text]], which produces: [[#GeoGroup|Example Link Text]]. == Expansion == Other KML aware services may be added, as they become available; as might services converting the coordinates to [[GPX]] or [[Point of interest#File formats|other formats]]. == Examples == {{GeoGroup{{\sandbox}}|section=Examples}} {| class="wikitable" ! (links to map & photo sources) |- | Dudley Port Junction | {{coord|52.52204|-2.04331|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Dudley Port Junction}} |- | Groveland Bridge (Tipton Road, A457) | {{coord|52.51758|-2.04760|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Groveland Bridge (Tipton Road, A457)}} |- | Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line) | {{coord|52.51640|-2.04843|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line)}} |- | Northern tunnel portal | {{coord|52.51534|-2.04948|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Northern tunnel portal}} |- | Aston Road air vent | {{coord|52.50850|-2.05518|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Aston Road air vent}} |- | Regent Road air vent | {{coord|52.50697|-2.05708|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Regent Road air vent}} |- | Approx. tunnel mid-point | {{coord|52.50435|-2.05932|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Approx. tunnel mid-point}} |- | Southern tunnel portal | {{coord|52.49335|-2.06916|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Southern tunnel portal}} |- | Windmill End Junction | {{coord|52.49157|-2.07019|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Windmill End Junction}} |} ===try1=== {{GeoGroup|section=Examples}} Try that differently, not using sandbox version ===try1.2=== {{GeoGroup{{List of United States cities by population}}|section=United States}} ===try1.3=== {{GeoGroup{{List of United States cities by population}}|section=Puerto Rico}} ===try2=== {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=Category:Rail transport stations in London fare zone 2}} Display all coordinates in [[:Category:Rail transport stations in London fare zone 2]] ===try3=== labelling above Display all coordinates in "United States" section of [[List of United States cities by population]] {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=[[List of United States cities by population}} {{GeoGroup{{\sandbox}}|section=United States}} ===try4=== {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=List of United States cities by population#Puerto Rico}} labelling below Display all coordinates in "Puerto Rico" section of [[List of United States cities by population]] ===try5=== Links may be made directly to a page's instance of the template, with the ID GeoGroup, by using [[#GeoGroup|Example Link Text]], which produces: [[#GeoGroup|Example Link Text]]. {{GeoGroup|This page's coordinates but with link wording changed}} ===try6=== {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=List of United States cities by population}} ===try7=== {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=List of United States cities by population|List of United States cities by population}} ===try8=== {{GeoGroup|article=List of United States cities by population|List of United States cities by population}} == Bookmarklet to add GeoGroup to categories == To added the template to categories, you may want to use the [[bookmarklet]] below. Sample edit: [http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Category:Loughs_of_County_Mayo&diff=230771016&oldid=165713411] javascript:with(document.forms[0]){void(wpSummary.value+='+{{[[Template:GeoGroup|GeoGroup]]}}%20(see%20also%20[[Wikipedia:WikiProject_Geographical_coordinates|WP:GEO]]');void(wpTextbox1.value='{{GeoGroup}}'+wpTextbox1.value);void(wpSave.click())} {{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|sandbox | | [[Category:External link templates]] [[Category:Map formatting and function templates]] [[Category:Geographic coordinate lists|τ]] [[Category:Articles with Geo|τ]] }}

revision.comment: add and try more as figure this out
revise to more reasonable set of examples
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{tl|GeoGroup}} generates an infobox type list of external links to services that present or display all [[Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] listed on certain Wikipedia articles that have multiples of coordinates. The template is also capable of generating all the title coordinates from many articles listed in a single Wikipedia category. The template is intended to be inserted in the ''External links'' section of articles, or inside a section with a list or table of features, if appropriate (e.g. see [[Tame Valley Canal]]). {{TOCright}} The linked services allow the following results: * A link to an [[Open Street Map]] map with the waypoints plotted onto the map. * A link to [[Bing]] map with the waypoints plotted onto the map. Note that if the page or category has more than 200 coordinates, not all will be plotted. Bing will present just the first 200 points. * Export all coordinates from the Wikipedia page or category in a [[Keyhole Markup Language|KML]] file suitable for use in [[Google Earth]], [[NASA World Wind]] and similar applications. * Export all coordinates in a GeoRSS file. * Export all coordinates in a GPX file. * Export Microformatted coordinates from the Wikipedia page in a [[GeoRSS]] file. * Export place data in an RDF file. {{TOCright}} ==Usage== Inline coordinates can be associated with a name using {{Tl|Coord}} with the {{para|name}} parameter, such as: :{{[[Template:Coord|coord]]|1|2|type:landmark|name=Interesting uncharted point in the sea}} However, do not use the {{para|name}} parameter when the coordinates are within a parent template or table which emits a [[Wikipedia:microformats|microformat]], such as an infobox. This template is not intended for articles with single sets of coordinates; such coordinates already link indirectly to {{Tl|GeoTemplate}}. To use this template, add {{tl|GeoGroup}} to the ''External links'' section of an article, or, for list articles with no title coordinate, at the top of the article. === Optional parameters === '''article=''' The name of an article or a category, from which coordinates will be displayed: :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=article=''article name''}} :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=article=''Category:category name''}} For a category example, see [[List of stations in London fare zone 2#List of stations]]) '''articlee=''' Encoded name of the article. The encoding process here involves changing spaces to underscores ( '''_''' ), everything else is taken care of by the [[Wikipedia:Magic words#Paths|urlencode magic word]]. :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=article=''article name''|3=articlee=''article_name''}} '''section=''' The section parameter arranges most of the links to use only coordinates in the named section of the article. This is useful to limit the number seen at one time in articles with many coordinates. For examples, see [[List of rapids of the Columbia River]]. :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=section=''section name''}} A section-specific GeoGroup makes most sense placed within the section to which it refers. No matter if the template is set to display coordinates of a faraway section, the GeoGroup box's wording will still assert it will display coordinates of "this section". Every section-specific GeoGroup box is worded "Map this section's coordinates...", and currently (March 2015) there is no way to change the wording presented. ===Option for categories=== There is one optional parameter for display of coordinates in categories: :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=level=''0,1,2,3,...''}} '''level=''' Category recursion level, where 0 means unlimited == Cache delay and updating maps == Currently (as of 18 March 2015) maps displayed using Open Street Map are regenerated with each visit, and no [[Web cache|caching]] is used. So if an editor adds or changes coordinates in an article, then clicks on the GeoGroup template to "Map all coordinates using OSM", the OSM map displayed will immediately show the changed data. For Bing maps (and previously for Google maps which were also included in GeoGroup), pages are [[Web cache|cached]]. New visits to a map corresponding to a Wikipedia article will, by default, continue to use any already-cached data (that has been stored from your last visit, or perhaps from some other Wikipedia user's recent visit to the map). The cache may not be cleared for several hours, and only then would a visit yield an updated map. Updating can be forced easily, however. Simply alter the [[url]] in the browser to override the Bing's default choice to use cached data: :When a Bing map is displayed, the URL shown in your browser's "searchurl line" (usually at top left of your browser window), will likely show something like: ::''http://www.bing.com/maps/?mapurl=http%3A%2F%2Ftools.wmflabs.org%2Fkmlexport%2F%3Farticle%3DCurrent_article_name%26usecache%3D1'' :Simply use your backspace or delete key to erase the "use cache" default choice at the end, so what appears is: ::''http://www.bing.com/maps/?mapurl=http%3A%2F%2Ftools.wmflabs.org%2Fkmlexport%2F%3Farticle%3DCurrent_article_name'' :Then hit return and an updated map will be displayed. :Or, simply change the last character from "1" to "0", telling Bing not to use cached data, so what appears is: ::''http://www.bing.com/maps/?mapurl=http%3A%2F%2Ftools.wmflabs.org%2Fkmlexport%2F%3Farticle%3DCurrent_article_name%26usecache%3D0'' :Then hit return and an updated map will be displayed. Or if what is displayed for URL is in plain text try: :Replace ''usecache=1'' by ''usecache=0'' instead. == Linking == Wiki-links may be made directly to a page's instance of the template, with the ID GeoGroup., by using [[#GeoGroup|Example Link Text]], which produces: [[#GeoGroup|Example Link Text]]. To jump to GeoGroup box location in the current article use: ::[[#GeoGroup|Jump to map box]], which produces: [[#GeoGroup|Jump to map box]]. To jump to GeoGroup box location in a different article use: ::[[Article name#GeoGroup|Example Link Text]] For example, [[Listed buildings in Runcorn (urban area)#GeoGroup|Mapping of Runcorn listed buildings]] produces: [[Listed buildings in Runcorn (urban area)#GeoGroup|Mapping of Runcorn listed buildings]] If there are multiple usages of GeoGroup template in an article, as in [[List of rapids of the Columbia River]], the target is not well-defined. For example, [[List of rapids of the Columbia River#GeoGroup|Jump to a map box of Columbia River rapids]] which produces [[List of rapids of the Columbia River#GeoGroup|Jump to a map box of Columbia River rapids]], could perhaps jump to any one of the GeoGroup templates in that article. == Expansion == Other KML aware services may be added, as they become available; as might services converting the coordinates to [[GPX]] ({{done}}) or [[Point of interest#File formats|other formats]]. == Examples == {{GeoGroup{{\sandbox}}|section=Examples}} {| class="wikitable" ! Point ! [[Geographic coordinate system|Coordinates]]
(links to map & photo sources) |- | Dudley Port Junction | {{coord|52.52204|-2.04331|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Dudley Port Junction}} |- | Groveland Bridge (Tipton Road, A457) | {{coord|52.51758|-2.04760|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Groveland Bridge (Tipton Road, A457)}} |- | Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line) | {{coord|52.51640|-2.04843|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line)}} |- | Northern tunnel portal | {{coord|52.51534|-2.04948|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Northern tunnel portal}} |- | Aston Road air vent | {{coord|52.50850|-2.05518|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Aston Road air vent}} |- | Regent Road air vent | {{coord|52.50697|-2.05708|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Regent Road air vent}} |- | Approx. tunnel mid-point | {{coord|52.50435|-2.05932|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Approx. tunnel mid-point}} |- | Southern tunnel portal | {{coord|52.49335|-2.06916|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Southern tunnel portal}} |- | Windmill End Junction | {{coord|52.49157|-2.07019|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Windmill End Junction}} |} {{clear}} ===Section examples=== Display the coordinates in a different section than this one (in current article): {{GeoGroup|section=Examples}} {{GeoGroup|section=Examples}} Result: {{clear}} Display the coordinates in a different section than this one (in current article), which has no coordinates: {{GeoGroup|section=Linking}} {{GeoGroup|section=Linking}} Result: {{clear}} Try to display the coordinates in a section of a different article: {{GeoGroup|article=List of United States cities by population|section=Puerto Rico}} {{GeoGroup|article=List of United States cities by population|section=Puerto Rico}} Result: Failure, as all coordinates in the article are displayed, not just the ones from the intended section. {{clear}} Try to display all coordinates in just one section of a differnt article, by giving a link to that section in the article: {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=List of United States cities by population#Puerto Rico}} {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=List of United States cities by population#Puerto Rico}} Result: Failure, as all coordinates in the article are displayed, not just the ones from the [[List of United States cities by population#Puerto Rico]] section. {{clear}} Display all coordinates in a small category: {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=Category:Rail transport stations in London fare zone 2}} {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=Category:Rail transport stations in London fare zone 2}} {{clear}} Display all coordinates in a larger category and all of its subcategories (using recursion level 0): {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=Category:Rail transport stations in London by fare zone|level=0}} {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=Category:Category:Rail transport stations in London by fare zone|level=0}} {{clear}} Display all coordinates in a largerr category and subcategories (using recursion level 1): {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=Category:Rail transport stations in London by fare zone|level=1}} {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=Category:Category:Rail transport stations in London by fare zone|level=1}} {{clear}} Display coordinates in a larger category and subcategories (using recursion level 2): {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=Category:Rail transport stations in London by fare zone|level=2}} {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=Category:Category:Rail transport stations in London by fare zone|level=2}} {{clear}} Display all coordinates in a much large category and all its subcategories: {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=Category:Railway stations in London|level=0}} {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=Category:Railway stations in London}} {{clear}} Can one use "GeoGroupTemplate"... {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=List of United States cities by population}} {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=List of United States cities by population}} {{clear}} instead of using "GeoGroup"? {{GeoGroup|article=List of United States cities by population}} {{GeoGroup|article=List of United States cities by population}} {{clear}} ===Wording displayed=== {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=Category:Rail transport stations in London fare zone 2}} There is currently no option to change the wording that is shown in the GeoGroup box displayed in an article. For example, when providing means for a reader to map locations within [[:Category:Rail transport stations in London fare zone 2]], an editor might prefer for the Geogroup box to display "Map the Zone 2 stations using OSM" in the article, instead of "Map all coordinates in 'Category:Rail transport stations in London fare zone 2' using OSM". However there is no way currently to change the wording presented. Also when "|section=" option is used, there is no way to change displayed wording from "Map this section's coordinates using OSM", etc. == Bookmarklet to add GeoGroup to categories == To added the template to categories, you may want to use the [[bookmarklet]] below. Sample edit: [http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Category:Loughs_of_County_Mayo&diff=230771016&oldid=165713411] javascript:with(document.forms[0]){void(wpSummary.value+='+{{[[Template:GeoGroup|GeoGroup]]}}%20(see%20also%20[[Wikipedia:WikiProject_Geographical_coordinates|WP:GEO]]');void(wpTextbox1.value='{{GeoGroup}}'+wpTextbox1.value);void(wpSave.click())} {{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|sandbox | | [[Category:External link templates]] [[Category:Map formatting and function templates]] [[Category:Geographic coordinate lists|τ]] [[Category:Articles with Geo|τ]] }}

To use this template, add {{tl|GeoGroup}} to the ''External links'' section of an article, or, for list articles with no title coordinate, at the top of the article. === Optional parameters === '''article=''' The name of an article or a category, from which coordinates will be displayed: :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=article=''article name''}} :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=article=''Category:category name''}} For a category example, see [[List of stations in London fare zone 2#List of stations]]) '''articlee=''' Encoded name of the article. The encoding process here involves changing spaces to underscores ( '''_''' ), everything else is taken care of by the [[Wikipedia:Magic words#Paths|urlencode magic word]]. :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=article=''article name''|3=articlee=''article_name''}} '''section=''' The section parameter arranges most of the links to use only coordinates in the named section of the article. This is useful to limit the number seen at one time in articles with many coordinates. For examples, see [[List of rapids of the Columbia River]]. :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=section=''section name''}} A section-specific GeoGroup makes most sense placed within the section to which it refers. No matter if the template is set to display coordinates of a faraway section, the GeoGroup box's wording will still assert it will display coordinates of "this section". Every section-specific GeoGroup box is worded "Map this section's coordinates...", and currently (March 2015) there is no way to change the wording presented. ===Option for categories=== There is one optional parameter for display of coordinates in categories: :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=level=''0,1,2,3,...''}} '''level=''' Category recursion level, where 0 means unlimited == Cache delay and updating maps == Currently (as of 18 March 2015) maps displayed using Open Street Map are regenerated with each visit, and no [[Web cache|caching]] is used. So if an editor adds or changes coordinates in an article, then clicks on the GeoGroup template to "Map all coordinates using OSM", the OSM map displayed will immediately show the changed data. Simply alter the [[url]] in the browser to override the Bing's default choice to use cached data: :When a Bing map is displayed, the URL shown in your browser's "searchurl line" (usually at top left of your browser window), will likely show something like: ::''http://www.bing.com/maps/?mapurl=http%3A%2F%2Ftools.wmflabs.org%2Fkmlexport%2F%3Farticle%3DCurrent_article_name%26usecache%3D1'' :Simply use your backspace or delete key to erase the "use cache" default choice at the end, so what appears is: ::''http://www.bing.com/maps/?mapurl=http%3A%2F%2Ftools.wmflabs.org%2Fkmlexport%2F%3Farticle%3DCurrent_article_name'' :Then hit return and an updated map will be displayed. :Or, simply change the last character from "1" to "0", telling Bing not to use cached data, so what appears is: ::''http://www.bing.com/maps/?mapurl=http%3A%2F%2Ftools.wmflabs.org%2Fkmlexport%2F%3Farticle%3DCurrent_article_name%26usecache%3D0'' :Then hit return and an updated map will be displayed. Or if what is displayed for URL is in plain text try: :Replace ''usecache=1'' by ''usecache=0'' instead. == Linking == Wiki-links may be made directly to a page's instance of the template, with the ID GeoGroup., by using [[#GeoGroup|Example Link Text]], which produces: [[#GeoGroup|Example Link Text]]. To jump to GeoGroup box location in a different article use: ::[[Article name#GeoGroup|Example Link Text]] For example, [[Listed buildings in Runcorn (urban area)#GeoGroup|Mapping of Runcorn listed buildings]] produces: [[Listed buildings in Runcorn (urban area)#GeoGroup|Mapping of Runcorn listed buildings]] If there are multiple usages of GeoGroup template in an article, as in [[List of rapids of the Columbia River]], the target is not well-defined. Point ! [[Geographic coordinate system|Coordinates]]
(links to map & photo sources) |- | Dudley Port Junction | {{coord|52.52204|-2.04331|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Dudley Port Junction}} |- | Groveland Bridge (Tipton Road, A457) | {{coord|52.51758|-2.04760|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Groveland Bridge (Tipton Road, A457)}} |- | Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line) | {{coord|52.51640|-2.04843|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line)}} |- | Northern tunnel portal | {{coord|52.51534|-2.04948|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Northern tunnel portal}} |- | Aston Road air vent | {{coord|52.50850|-2.05518|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Aston Road air vent}} |- | Regent Road air vent | {{coord|52.50697|-2.05708|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Regent Road air vent}} |- | Approx. tunnel mid-point | {{coord|52.50435|-2.05932|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Approx. tunnel mid-point}} |- | Southern tunnel portal | {{coord|52.49335|-2.06916|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Southern tunnel portal}} |- | Windmill End Junction | {{coord|52.49157|-2.07019|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Windmill End Junction}} |} {{clear}} ===Section examples=== Display the coordinates in a different section than this one (in current article): {{GeoGroup|section=Examples}} {{GeoGroup|section=Examples}} Result: Works {{clear}} Display the coordinates in a different section than this one (in current article), which has no coordinates: {{GeoGroup|section=Linking}} {{GeoGroup|section=Linking}} Result: Works: "sorry, no data to show" {{clear}} Display all the coordinates in a different article: {{GeoGroup|article=List of United States cities by population}} {{GeoGroup|article=List of United States cities by population}} Result: Works {{clear}} Display just the coordinates in one section of a different article: {{GeoGroup|article=List of United States cities by population|section=Puerto Rico}} {{GeoGroup|article=List of United States cities by population|section=Puerto Rico}} Result: Works {{clear}} Try to display all coordinates in just one section of a different article, by giving a link to that section in the article: {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=List of United States cities by population#Puerto Rico}} {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=List of United States cities by population#Puerto Rico}} Result: Fails, as all coordinates in the article are displayed, not just the ones from the [[List of United States cities by population#Puerto Rico]] section. {{clear}} Display all coordinates in a small category: {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=Category:Rail transport stations in London fare zone 2}} {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=Category:Rail transport stations in London fare zone 2}} {{clear}} Display all coordinates in a larger category and all of its subcategories (using recursion level 0): {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=Category:Rail transport stations in London by fare zone|level=0}} {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=Category:Category:Rail transport stations in London by fare zone|level=0}} {{clear}} Display all coordinates in a largerr category and subcategories (using recursion level 1): {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=Category:Rail transport stations in London by fare zone|level=1}} {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=Category:Category:Rail transport stations in London by fare zone|level=1}} {{clear}} Display coordinates in a larger category and subcategories (using recursion level 2): {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=Category:Rail transport stations in London by fare zone|level=2}} {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=Category:Category:Rail transport stations in London by fare zone|level=2}} {{clear}} Display all coordinates in a much large category and all its subcategories: {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=Category:Railway stations in London|level=0}} {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=Category:Railway stations in London}} {{clear}} Can one use "GeoGroupTemplate"... {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=List of United States cities by population}} {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=List of United States cities by population}} {{clear}} instead of using "GeoGroup"? {{GeoGroup|article=List of United States cities by population}} {{GeoGroup|article=List of United States cities by population}} {{clear}} ===Wording displayed=== {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=Category:Rail transport stations in London fare zone 2}} There is currently no option to change the wording that is shown in the GeoGroup box displayed in an article. For example, when providing means for a reader to map locations within [[:Category:Rail transport stations in London fare zone 2]], an editor might prefer for the Geogroup box to display "Map the Zone 2 stations using OSM" in the article, instead of "Map all coordinates in 'Category:Rail transport stations in London fare zone 2' using OSM". However there is no way currently to change the wording presented. Also when "|section=" option is used, there is no way to change displayed wording from "Map this section's coordinates using OSM", etc. == Bookmarklet to add GeoGroup to categories == To added the template to categories, you may want to use the [[bookmarklet]] below. The template is also capable of generating all the title coordinates from many articles listed in a single Wikipedia category. Note that if the page or category has more than 200 coordinates, not all will be plotted. Bing will present just the first 200 points. * Export all coordinates from the Wikipedia page or category in a [[Keyhole Markup Language|KML]] file suitable for use in [[Google Earth]], [[NASA World Wind]] and similar applications. * Export all coordinates in a GeoRSS file. * Export all coordinates in a GPX file. * Export Microformatted coordinates from the Wikipedia page in a [[GeoRSS]] file. * Export place data in an RDF file. {{TOCright}} ==Usage== Inline coordinates can be associated with a name using {{Tl|Coord}} with the {{para|name}} parameter, such as: :{{[[Template:Coord|coord]]|1|2|type:landmark|name=Interesting uncharted point in the sea}} However, do not use the {{para|name}} parameter when the coordinates are within a parent template or table which emits a [[Wikipedia:microformats|microformat]], such as an infobox. This template is not intended for articles with single sets of coordinates; such coordinates already link indirectly to {{Tl|GeoTemplate}}. To use this template, add {{tl|GeoGroup}} to the ''External links'' section of an article, or, for list articles with no title coordinate, at the top of the article. === Optional parameters === '''article=''' The name of an article or a category, from which coordinates will be displayed: :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=article=''article name''}} :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=article=''Category:category name''}} For a category example, see [[List of stations in London fare zone 2#List of stations]]) '''articlee=''' Encoded name of the article. The encoding process here involves changing spaces to underscores ( '''_''' ), everything else is taken care of by the [[Wikipedia:Magic words#Paths|urlencode magic word]]. :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=article=''article name''|3=articlee=''article_name''}} '''section=''' The section parameter arranges most of the links to use only coordinates in the named section of the article. This is useful to limit the number seen at one time in articles with many coordinates. For examples, see [[List of rapids of the Columbia River]]. :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=section=''section name''}} A section-specific GeoGroup makes most sense placed within the section to which it refers. No matter if the template is set to display coordinates of a faraway section, the GeoGroup box's wording will still assert it will display coordinates of "this section". Every section-specific GeoGroup box is worded "Map this section's coordinates...", and currently (March 2015) there is no way to change the wording presented. ===Option for categories=== There is one optional parameter for display of coordinates in categories: :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=level=''0,1,2,3,...''}} '''level=''' Category recursion level, where 0 means unlimited == Cache delay and updating maps == Currently (as of 18 March 2015) maps displayed using Open Street Map are regenerated with each visit, and no [[Web cache|caching]] is used. So if an editor adds or changes coordinates in an article, then clicks on the GeoGroup template to "Map all coordinates using OSM", the OSM map displayed will immediately show the changed data. For Bing maps (and previously for Google maps which were also included in GeoGroup), pages are [[Web cache|cached]]. New visits to a map corresponding to a Wikipedia article will, by default, continue to use any already-cached data (that has been stored from your last visit, or perhaps from some other Wikipedia user's recent visit to the map). The cache may not be cleared for several hours, and only then would a visit yield an updated map. Updating can be forced easily, however. Simply alter the [[url]] in the browser to override the Bing's default choice to use cached data: :When a Bing map is displayed, the URL shown in your browser's "searchurl line" (usually at top left of your browser window), will likely show something like: ::''http://www.bing.com/maps/?mapurl=http%3A%2F%2Ftools.wmflabs.org%2Fkmlexport%2F%3Farticle%3DCurrent_article_name%26usecache%3D1'' :Simply use your backspace or delete key to erase the "use cache" default choice at the end, so what appears is: ::''http://www.bing.com/maps/?mapurl=http%3A%2F%2Ftools.wmflabs.org%2Fkmlexport%2F%3Farticle%3DCurrent_article_name'' :Then hit return and an updated map will be displayed. :Or, simply change the last character from "1" to "0", telling Bing not to use cached data, so what appears is: ::''http://www.bing.com/maps/?mapurl=http%3A%2F%2Ftools.wmflabs.org%2Fkmlexport%2F%3Farticle%3DCurrent_article_name%26usecache%3D0'' :Then hit return and an updated map will be displayed. Or if what is displayed for URL is in plain text try: :Replace ''usecache=1'' by ''usecache=0'' instead. == Linking == Wiki-links may be made directly to a page's instance of the template, with the ID GeoGroup., by using [[#GeoGroup|Example Link Text]], which produces: [[#GeoGroup|Example Link Text]]. To jump to GeoGroup box location in the current article use: ::[[#GeoGroup|Jump to map box]], which produces: [[#GeoGroup|Jump to map box]]. To jump to GeoGroup box location in a different article use: ::[[Article name#GeoGroup|Example Link Text]] For example, [[Listed buildings in Runcorn (urban area)#GeoGroup|Mapping of Runcorn listed buildings]] produces: [[Listed buildings in Runcorn (urban area)#GeoGroup|Mapping of Runcorn listed buildings]] If there are multiple usages of GeoGroup template in an article, as in [[List of rapids of the Columbia River]], the target is not well-defined. For example, [[List of rapids of the Columbia River#GeoGroup|Jump to a map box of Columbia River rapids]] which produces [[List of rapids of the Columbia River#GeoGroup|Jump to a map box of Columbia River rapids]], could perhaps jump to any one of the GeoGroup templates in that article. == Expansion == Other KML aware services may be added, as they become available; as might services converting the coordinates to [[GPX]] ({{done}}) or [[Point of interest#File formats|other formats]]. == Examples == ===Netherton Tunnel Branch Canal=== Data from [[Netherton Tunnel Branch Canal]] article: {{GeoGroup{{\sandbox}}|section=Examples}} {| class="wikitable" ! Point ! [[Geographic coordinate system|Coordinates]]
(links to map & photo sources) |- | Dudley Port Junction | {{coord|52.52204|-2.04331|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Dudley Port Junction}} |- | Groveland Bridge (Tipton Road, A457) | {{coord|52.51758|-2.04760|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Groveland Bridge (Tipton Road, A457)}} |- | Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line) | {{coord|52.51640|-2.04843|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Tividale Aqueduct (Old BCN Main Line)}} |- | Northern tunnel portal | {{coord|52.51534|-2.04948|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Northern tunnel portal}} |- | Aston Road air vent | {{coord|52.50850|-2.05518|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Aston Road air vent}} |- | Regent Road air vent | {{coord|52.50697|-2.05708|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Regent Road air vent}} |- | Approx. tunnel mid-point | {{coord|52.50435|-2.05932|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Approx. tunnel mid-point}} |- | Southern tunnel portal | {{coord|52.49335|-2.06916|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Southern tunnel portal}} |- | Windmill End Junction | {{coord|52.49157|-2.07019|region:GB_type:landmark|name=Windmill End Junction}} |} {{clear}} ===Other examples=== {{GeoGroup|section=Examples}} Display the coordinates from a different section than this one (in current article): {{GeoGroup|section=Netherton Tunnel Branch Canal}} Result: Works as intended, but GeoGroup box displays "this section". {{clear}} {{GeoGroup|section=Linking}} Display the coordinates from a different section than this one (in current article), when that section has no coordinates: {{GeoGroup|section=Linking}} Result: Works: "sorry, no data to show", but GeoGroup box displays "this section". {{clear}} {{GeoGroup|article=List of United States cities by population}} Display all the coordinates in a different article: {{GeoGroup|article=List of United States cities by population}} Result: Works {{clear}} {{GeoGroup|article=List of United States cities by population|section=Puerto Rico}} Display just the coordinates in one section of a different article: {{GeoGroup|article=List of United States cities by population|section=Puerto Rico}} Result: Works, but GeoGroup box displays "this section". {{clear}} {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=List of United States cities by population#Puerto Rico}} Try to display all coordinates in just one section of a different article, by giving a link to that section in the article: {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=List of United States cities by population#Puerto Rico}} Result: Fails, as all coordinates in the article are displayed, not just the ones from the [[List of United States cities by population#Puerto Rico|intended section]]. {{clear}} {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=Category:Rail transport stations in London fare zone 2}} Display all coordinates in a category: {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=Category:Rail transport stations in London fare zone 2}} Result: Works {{clear}} {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=Category:Category:Rail transport stations in London by fare zone|level=0}} Try to display all coordinates in a larger category and all of its subcategories (using recursion level 0): {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=Category:Rail transport stations in London by fare zone|level=0}} Result: Fails {{clear}} {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=Category:Category:Rail transport stations in London by fare zone|level=1}} Display all coordinates in a larger category and subcategories (using recursion level 1): {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=Category:Rail transport stations in London by fare zone|level=1}} Result: Fails {{clear}} {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=Category:Category:Rail transport stations in London by fare zone|level=2}} Display coordinates in a larger category and subcategories (using recursion level 2): {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=Category:Rail transport stations in London by fare zone|level=2}} Result: Fails {{clear}} Display all coordinates in a much large category and all its subcategories: {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=Category:Railway stations in London|level=0}} {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=Category:Railway stations in London}} {{clear}} Note one can use "GeoGroupTemplate" or "GeoGroup" interchangeably. ===Wording displayed=== {{GeoGroupTemplate|article=Category:Rail transport stations in London fare zone 2}} There is currently no option to change the wording that is shown in the GeoGroup box displayed in an article. For example, when providing means for a reader to map locations within [[:Category:Rail transport stations in London fare zone 2]], an editor might prefer for the Geogroup box to display "Map the Zone 2 stations using OSM" in the article, instead of "Map all coordinates in 'Category:Rail transport stations in London fare zone 2' using OSM". However there is no way currently to change the wording presented. Also when "|section=" option is used, there is no way to change displayed wording from "Map this section's coordinates using OSM", etc. == Bookmarklet to add GeoGroup to categories == To added the template to categories, you may want to use the [[bookmarklet]] below. Sample edit: [http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Category:Loughs_of_County_Mayo&diff=230771016&oldid=165713411] javascript:with(document.forms[0]){void(wpSummary.value+='+{{[[Template:GeoGroup|GeoGroup]]}}%20(see%20also%20[[Wikipedia:WikiProject_Geographical_coordinates|WP:GEO]]');void(wpTextbox1.value='{{GeoGroup}}'+wpTextbox1.value);void(wpSave.click())} {{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|sandbox | | [[Category:External link templates]] [[Category:Map formatting and function templates]] [[Category:Geographic coordinate lists|τ]] [[Category:Articles with Geo|τ]] }}

{{Documentation subpage}} {{tl|GeoGroup}} generates an infobox type list of external links to services that present or display all [[Geographic coordinate system|coordinates]] listed on certain Wikipedia articles that have multiples of coordinates. The template is also capable of generating all the title coordinates from many articles listed in a single Wikipedia category. The template is intended to be inserted in the ''External links'' section of articles, or inside a section with a list or table of features, if appropriate (e.g. see [[Tame Valley Canal]]). {{TOCright}} The linked services allow the following results: * A link to an [[Open Street Map]] map with the waypoints plotted onto the map. * A link to [[Bing]] map with the waypoints plotted onto the map. Note that if the page or category has more than 200 coordinates, not all will be plotted. Bing will present just the first 200 points. * Export all coordinates from the Wikipedia page or category in a [[Keyhole Markup Language|KML]] file suitable for use in [[Google Earth]], [[NASA World Wind]] and similar applications. * Export all coordinates in a GeoRSS file. * Export all coordinates in a GPX file. * Export Microformatted coordinates from the Wikipedia page in a [[GeoRSS]] file. * Export place data in an RDF file. {{TOCright}} ==Usage== Inline coordinates can be associated with a name using {{Tl|Coord}} with the {{para|name}} parameter, such as: :{{[[Template:Coord|coord]]|1|2|type:landmark|name=Interesting uncharted point in the sea}} However, do not use the {{para|name}} parameter when the coordinates are within a parent template or table which emits a [[Wikipedia:microformats|microformat]], such as an infobox. The encoding process here involves changing spaces to underscores ( '''_''' ), everything else is taken care of by the [[Wikipedia:Magic words#Paths|urlencode magic word]]. :{{tlx|GeoGroup|2=article=''article name''|3=articlee=''article_name''}} '''section=''' The section parameter arranges most of the links to use only coordinates in the named section of the article. New visits to a map corresponding to a Wikipedia article will, by default, continue to use any already-cached data (that has been stored from your last visit, or perhaps from some other Wikipedia user's recent visit to the map). Simply alter the [[url]] in the browser to override the Bing's default choice to use cached data: :When a Bing map is displayed, the URL shown in your browser's "searchurl line" (usually at top left of your browser window), will likely show something like: ::''http://www.bing.com/maps/?mapurl=http%3A%2F%2Ftools.wmflabs.org%2Fkmlexport%2F%3Farticle%3DCurrent_article_name%26usecache%3D1'' :Simply use your backspace or delete key to erase the "use cache" default choice at the end, so what appears is: ::''http://www.bing.com/maps/?mapurl=http%3A%2F%2Ftools.wmflabs.org%2Fkmlexport%2F%3Farticle%3DCurrent_article_name'' :Then hit return and an updated map will be displayed. :Or, simply change the last character from "1" to "0", telling Bing not to use cached data, so what appears is: ::''http://www.bing.com/maps/?mapurl=http%3A%2F%2Ftools.wmflabs.org%2Fkmlexport%2F%3Farticle%3DCurrent_article_name%26usecache%3D0'' :Then hit return and an updated map will be displayed.
